programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Confidential files are leaked by an anonymous hacker, who threatens to release more unless the Navy agrees to renew a contract with a struggling shipbuilding firm. The Navy Secretary suspects the company's owner Philip Wickes is to blame, and asks NCIS to join forces with the PsyOps unit in an elaborate ruse to bring the culprit out of hiding. Meanwhile, Gibbs' colleagues begin to notice his attraction to his opposite number Samantha Ryan. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A Navy officer dies following a confrontation involving a dancer and a Sudanese emigre at a strip club. However, when investigations reveal he was a code-breaker involved in naval intelligence, Callen and Sam dig deeper and gradually unearth links to Safar, a Libyan-born terrorist known to recruit on behalf of al-Qaeda. Henri Lubatti guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Clumsy Clown

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy lieutenant is found dead in the Pentagon's custodial closet, and surveillance footage indicates she was killed by a co-worker. However, Ziva and the team discover her supposed assailant actually died of a heart attack days before the crime was committed. Meanwhile, suspicions are raised regarding the eyeball left in Tony's drink, and the investigators' attention is fixed on the port-to-port murderer, who appears to still be at large.(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A taxi driver recently released from prison is murdered just hours after sending an e-mail to an old alias that Callen used when he went undercover to investigate three marines who stole $5million. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A model dies while filming a reality TV show at a US Marine base, and an autopsy reveals she was killed by a drug overdose administered by someone else. Inquiries lead the team to identify three possible suspects - but one of them is also murdered. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Lauren Holly.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony and Ziva disappear while searching for smuggled weapons at a shipyard. Gibbs is soon on the case and learns the pair were looking for a freight container thought to hold explosives from Iraq. McGee tries to get a GPS trace on the missing duo's mobiles, leaving the others struggling to find them before they come to serious harm. Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A commander who knows the location of a shipment of nuclear fuel rods is abducted, and the team is called in to try to identify the kidnapper. However, as the investigation turns up new evidence, it appears someone might have had a very different motive for silencing the officer. Guest starring Zac Efron.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The wives of two marines are murdered, and although Gibbs initially suspects they were victims of domestic violence, subsequent developments suggest the case may involve professional killers who already have their next victim in mind. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion must rescue a woman trapped in the La Brea Tar Pits before she runs out of oxygen, while Paige's mother Veronica maximises Ralph's troop cookie sales.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team encounters gunrunners while spending Christmas Eve at an electronics-free mountain getaway, and Tim makes a decision about his place in the unit.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo teams up with his girlfriend, ATF agent Zoe Keates, to investigate the circumstances surrounding the death of a US Marine in what initially seems like a simple road accident. The team reveals the dead man did indeed die due to a broken neck sustained during a crash, but someone shot at him first. As DiNozzo and Keates pursue their inquiry, they discover the victim's death is connected to a controversial ATF plan to place traceable firearms in the criminal system, and then follow them to the heads of crime organisations. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US marine visits the home of his former captain, only to find the man has been killed, and the killer is waiting inside the property. While the soldier escapes with his life, the attacker gets away too. It quickly emerges the prime suspect is Qasim, an Afghan translator evidence, and evidence points against him, until it is discovered he has a brother, who is loyal to the Taliban. The subsequent chain of evidence leads Gibbs and Bishop to Afghanistan.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Naval intelligence ensign is killed in an unusual car accident. Gibbs and his team are reunited with old friend Ned Dorneget, who is now working with NCIS cyber operations, and NSA attorney Jake Malloy to investigate the case, and discover a conspiracy involving terrorist recruitment on the internet.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Special Agent Ned Dorneget of the NCIS cyber division joins the team to further their investigation into The Calling, the insidious terror network radicalising youngsters via the internet. When it emerges young suicide bomber Bradley was not, in fact, affiliated with The Calling, Gibbs and his colleagues realise they might be back to square-one. Determined to bring the organisation down, the agents pursue two new leads, an arms dealer who may be supplying The Calling with their signature German S-mines, and another boy given a deadly mission by the terrorists. Guest starring Matt Jones.(n)

programme.desc: A Navy reservist is found dead in his home with marks around his neck, and Gibbs and the team discover he was working on a highly classified case for the department of defence. programme.desc: Gina is reprimanded by Amanda Prosser for her drunken behaviour, but redeems herself by devising an impressive new initiative against antisocial behaviour. programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The grand opening of Pryde Castle ends in disaster when a guide is struck by an arrow, and Father Brown becomes convinced that the motive lies buried in the Pryde family's past. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams as a Roman Catholic priest who attempts to solve various misdemeanours.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Convicted killer Violet is given a temporary reprieve from the gallows, and begs Father Brown to help prove her innocence. However, with only three days in which to do it, he must try to find the real culprit before it's too late. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance of an old friend leads Father Brown and Sid on a quest to find the mythical Lannington Rosary, and an old adversary, seemingly back from the dead, must be trusted to help. Mark Williams stars.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Armed only with a telescope, the priest hopes that a broken leg will not prevent him from solving the mystery surrounding the death of a member of Kembleford's Women's Institute. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams, Sorcha Cusack, Nancy Carroll and Tom Chambers.(n)

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: The body of a young police officer is found in the station and fear grips the town as nobody knows why the killer would target the police.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Richard Poole meets up with a group of old university friends who have arrived on the island for a reunion, only for tragedy to strike when the man who has solved so many murders becomes the victim. Faced with such a sensitive crime, the commissioner quickly drafts in another British inspector to help the team get to the bottom of the mystery. It soon becomes clear that the newcomer, Humphrey Goodman, is just as eccentric as Poole - but he is also just as bright, and wastes no time questioning the partygoers, all of whom are prime suspects, while pondering the biggest mystery of all - how did the culprit commit the crime in full view of all the other guests? Kris Marshall joins the cast, with Ben Miller, Sara Martins and Danny John-Jules.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A zombie movie being filmed on the island ends in real-life horror when a stand-in is poisoned - and new detective inspector Humphrey soon deduces that the unfortunate victim was not the killer's intended target. He believes the culprit was after lead actress Lexi Cunningham, but the murder plot went wrong. The Caribbean cops focus their attention on three suspects - film director Carl Collins, screenwriter Arnold Finch and production assistant Susie Jenkins. But why would any of them want the star dead? Michelle Ryan, Peter Davison and Hannah Tointon guest star.(n)

programme.desc: A woman becomes convinced she has contracted syphilis from her husband, who indignantly claims he has never had an affair - raising suspicions that she is the one who has been unfaithful. Starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie.(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques expert stumbles on what seems to be a bargain in Frankie's scrap yard in the shape of an iron cannon. Though convinced his luck must finally have changed, problems lie ahead when the item attracts the attention of a devious major. Tongue-in-cheek drama, with Ian McShane and guest star Leslie Phillips.(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Ram-raiders destroy a valuable Sheraton desk and steal what they haven't broken, forcing the roguish dealer to scout around for new stock. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan.(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer gets involved in a con at a country house involving the sale of a fake painting in exchange for a parcel of diamonds. However, Lovejoy later realises he too has been set up over the deal. Richard Griffiths guest stars, with Ian McShane.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Richard Poole meets up with a group of old university friends who have arrived on the island for a reunion, only for tragedy to strike when the man who has solved so many murders becomes the victim. Faced with such a sensitive crime, the commissioner quickly drafts in another British inspector to help the team get to the bottom of the mystery. It soon becomes clear that the newcomer, Humphrey Goodman, is just as eccentric as Poole - but he is also just as bright, and wastes no time questioning the partygoers, all of whom are prime suspects, while pondering the biggest mystery of all - how did the culprit commit the crime in full view of all the other guests? Kris Marshall joins the cast, with Ben Miller, Sara Martins and Danny John-Jules.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A zombie movie being filmed on the island ends in real-life horror when a stand-in is poisoned - and new detective inspector Humphrey soon deduces that the unfortunate victim was not the killer's intended target. He believes the culprit was after lead actress Lexi Cunningham, but the murder plot went wrong. The Caribbean cops focus their attention on three suspects - film director Carl Collins, screenwriter Arnold Finch and production assistant Susie Jenkins. But why would any of them want the star dead? Michelle Ryan, Peter Davison and Hannah Tointon guest star.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and the team are called upon to investigate the death of Fidel's old school friend, Carlton Paris, who has been shot at home. The victim was last seen alive at an art gallery with Dorothy Foster, a wealthy older woman - and before long they discover he made his living as an escort and had left several disgruntled and heartbroken women in his wake, including a local judge and the wife of a club owner. But as the investigation progresses, Fidel struggles to keep his emotions in check, forcing him to confront some long-buried issues. Kris Marshall and Gary Carr star, with guest appearances from Adrian Scarborough, Sharon Small, Tristan Gemmill and Josette Simon.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A flight attendant is poisoned during her crew's overnight stay on the island. Humphrey and his team are called to the victim's hotel and begin by questioning her colleagues, but with so many suspects and so little to go on, they are left scratching their heads trying to work out which of her colleagues wanted her dead - so Dwayne goes rogue to pursue his own line of inquiry. Kris Marshall and Danny John-Jules star in the light-hearted Caribbean crime drama.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The island's commerce minister is shot dead in his home, and with the incident coming shortly after the exposure of a scandalous affair - not to mention a suicide note at the scene - it looks like an open-and-shut case. But Humphrey isn't convinced and believes someone else was responsible for the killing, whittling down the possible suspects to leave his betrayed wife, his son and his mistress in the frame. The rest of the team struggles to see past the physical evidence, which still suggests he took his own life - and then matters are complicated even further when Camille gets a blast from the past.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A birdwatcher is stabbed to death with his own knife, a case that holds a certain fascination for Humphrey, who was a keen twitcher in his youth. The victim was one of a group of enthusiasts who had visited the island for a glimpse of a famous parrot - but the team soon faces a conundrum as all the suspects were in full sight of one another when the crime was committed. As the cops search for answers, they uncover evidence of long-standing rivalries, secret jealousies and romantic entanglements. But would any of it justify killing a harmless ornithologist?(n)

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Darkness Before The Dawn - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. A constable is shot in a botched raid on an opium den led by Watts, and Murdoch suspects John is being framed for murder by Inspector McWorthy.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.sub-title: The Man with the Golden Gun
programme.desc: Humphrey and his colleagues are called to a neighbouring private island whose property developer owner has been shot dead at a family gathering. Five people are in the frame - the victim's housekeeper, his PA and his three children - although when an approaching hurricane strands the cops on the island, knocking out the phones and computers, they are forced to rely on good old-fashioned investigative techniques. As evening approaches, the cops realise they will be spending the night in a house with a killer. Can they solve the mystery before he or she strikes again? Jimmy Akingbola (Holby's Antoine Malick) and Nikki Amuka-Bird (Luther) guest star.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: DI Goodman is called in to investigate the death of former surgeon Emma Redding at a retirement home. With an empty packet of sleeping pills beside her body and her room locked from the inside, it looks like an open-and-shut case of suicide. However, the team discovers everything is not as it seems and mounting evidence suggests she may in fact have been poisoned. Then, a blast from Humphrey's past arrives unexpectedly on the island, and looks set to distract him from solving the case. Guest starring Rupert Vansittart, Joanna David, Phil Davis and Michele Dotrice.(n)

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Back on the murder squad, Pat is asked to investigate when a young mother of two, Diane Leach, is abducted from a local wood. The confession of a local sex offender complicates matters, as Pat looks into the possibility that the father of a young protege of the woman may be responsible for her disappearance. Meanwhile, Walker has worries of his own, as his ex-wife Lyn is dating an abusive, unstable man, who turns into a manic-obsessive when she decides to leave him.(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.sub-title: A Rose by Any Other Name
programme.desc: A middle-aged widow's family become convinced a photographer is only marrying her for money and ask Hetty to investigate. Given a week to probe his past and find out whether he's a gold-digging cad or a 24-carat gentleman, the unlikely sleuth unearths some extremely interesting facts. Drama based on the novel by David Cook, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield.(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth disguises herself as a battered wife in an attempt to find out who is threatening the future of a women's refuge. Hetty's quest to find the leak opens her eyes to the terrifying realities of spousal abuse, and results in a gripping conclusion as she leads the women in defending their sanctuary against a violent motorcycle gang.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Acting Superintendent Amanda Prosser arrives at Sun Hill, where she makes a good impression on Jo Masters and Phil Hunter. Zain discovers Smithy is sleeping with Louise Larson, and warns him the consequences could be deadly if her husband finds out. June is forced to deal with a date-rape case on her first day back at work, while Yvonne resolves to admit she assaulted a drunk the night before - but reckons without Honey and Tony, who talk her out of it and destroy CCTV evidence of the attack.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: As her trial nears, Yvonne learns the drunk she recently assaulted has been called to give evidence against her, and tearfully tells Tony she is sure the prosecution have enough evidence to put her away for a long time. Amber is paired up with Honey and sent to find the man responsible for two date-rape incidents, but puts herself at risk trying to catch the suspect and almost becomes his latest victim. Phil beats up Steve for sleeping with Kate Maltby, while Zain introduces Smithy to an informant who has had two fingers chopped off by Pete Larson, and warns he runs the risk of losing a more intimate part of his anatomy if he's not careful.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jack takes a break from paperwork to help investigate a case alongside CID officers, only to discover his increasingly wayward son Ben may be the mastermind behind a major-league drugs operation. Yvonne's trial gets under way, and Laura Bryant begins work as a PC, while a sceptical Sheelagh kidnaps a youngster allegedly suffering from leukaemia, unwilling to believe there's anything wrong with her.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Yvonne finally learns her fate, and Jack Meadows continues to build a case against son Ben, but discovers to his frustration that he can only muster enough evidence to charge a comparatively insignificant drugs courier. Smithy tells Sheelagh that Gabriel raped Kerry Young, while Gina makes a bad impression on new boss Amanda Prosser by throwing a drink over a copper from Barton Street station.(n)

programme.title: VE Day 75: The People's Celebration
programme.desc: To mark the 75th anniversary of VE Day, the BBC and the Royal British Legion honour and celebrate the Second World War generation in a programme featuring recollections from some those who remember this historic day. Popular songs of the era are performed by stars including Katherine Jenkins, Adrian Lester, Anton Du Beke, Beverley Knight, Helen George, Sharon D Clarke, Shane Richie and Emma Barton. In a fitting finale at 9.00, the nation is asked to unite for a very special rendition of We'll Meet Again, as a gesture of thanks to the veterans and in memory of lost loved ones. Presented by Sophie Raworth.(n)

programme.title: VE Day: Remembering Victory
programme.desc: May 8, 2015 marked 70 years since Winston Churchill announced the Second World War in Europe was over, prompting Britain to throw the biggest street party the country has ever seen. David Attenborough, Bruce Forsyth, Miriam Margolyes and June Whitfield are just a few of the well-known figures sharing their memories of the end of the conflict. Narrated by Julie Walters, with contributions from others including Johnny Ball, Honor Blackman, Jilly Cooper, John Craven, Cleo Laine, Kenny Lynch, Michael Parkinson, Leslie Phillips, Anne Reid, Esther Rantzen, Patrick Stewart and Una Stubbs. First shown in 2015.(n)

There are tears plus lots of laughter as they tour a selection of top East Anglian properties. Away from the house hunt, Jules follows a paper trail to uncover an endangered decorative heritage craft.(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: The makers of an acne-treatment product, a family card game and a sport support top designed to prevent injury during exercise watch on while their items are consumer-tested.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a soup factory in Wigan that produces 2.5 million cans a day, and follows the production of a tin of vegetable soup from a pea harvest to the eventual dispatch. He also encounters a pressure cooker that stands 27.4 metres tall. Cherry Healey compares the vitamin content of fresh and frozen vegetables, and visits a salt mine in Crewe, while historian Ruth Goodman cooks up a butterted spinach soup using a recipe from a 17th-century cookbook, which is the earliest known reference to soup in English.(n)

programme.title: Home Front Heroes
programme.desc: Comedian Helen Lederer uncovers the ways in which two of her relatives took on pivotal but highly secretive Second World War roles assisting the British Intelligence Services. As she pieces together how her grandfather listened in on the secrets of imprisoned German officers, she's astonished to find out the true extent of his wartime work. Narrated by Siobhan Finneran.(n)

programme.title: The Man Who Never Was (1956)
programme.desc: Allied forces prepare to invade Europe through Sicily - but to throw their adversaries off their scent, they devise an ingenious and complicated plan to make the enemy believe the invasion will happen somewhere else. Fact-based Second World War drama, starring Clifton Webb, Gloria Grahame, Stephen Boyd, Robert Flemyng and Andre Morell.(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.sub-title: Paul Hayes v John Cameron Auction
programme.desc: In Warrington, Paul Hayes and John Cameron go head to head over a cameo brooch. Paul resorts to sneaky tactics in a bid to hide lots from his rival, but will his competitive strategy pay off?(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.sub-title: Jonty Hearnden vs Danny Sebastian - UK Antiques Fair
programme.desc: Jonty Hearnden and Danny Sebastian go head to head at the Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex. Danny thinks he's on track with a vintage railway sign and Jonty's got his eye on the prize with an antique telescope.(n)

programme.title: Scotland's VE Day: Countdown to Victory
programme.desc: Ahead of the 70th anniversary of VE Day on Friday, another chance to see a 2005 programme in which Scottish veterans of the Second World War reflect on the final week of the conflict. They reveal incredible tales of endurance, compassion and cruelty, including Scottish PoWs held hostage by their Russian 'allies', while at home an exhausted nation awaited the final German surrender.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Burton Constable Hall in the East Riding of Yorkshire, where antiques expert Jonathan Pratt finds a bronze statue and Catherine Southon enthuses about a silver bowl. Paul also finds out about the life of poet Philip Larkin.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon, Clive Myrie, Sara Pascoe and Rick Edwards compete in Double Points Friday, and their scores are tallied from the week resulting in an overall champion being crowned.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three inspirational farming families are celebrated for their ideas and ingenuity, but only one can be crowned Diversification Farmer of the Year at the Farmers Weekly Awards staged in London. Narrated by Denis Lawson.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef heads across one of the busiest border crossings in the world - San Diego to Tijuana - for the start of his Mexican adventures. He follows the Pacific coastline down to Ensenada, and samples flour tortilla burritos, fresh fish, marinated and cooked on Popotla Beach, and visits the Valle de Guadalupe - Mexico's best kept secret for fabulous wines.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The latest two highest-scoring North East chefs cook their six-course menus again to impress judges Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The arbitrators are joined by children's author Jacqueline Wilson, who has written over 100 novels since she started writing books at the age of nine. Presented by Susan Calman.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Rachel de Thame reviews the progress of her new kitchen garden, and begins planting out peas and sowing flower seeds. Nick Bailey shows how to make an easy cold frame for storing precious plants and seedlings, and Joe Swift meets Will Young, whose new-found love of gardening was key to his recovery from mental health problems.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Jason Manford, Jimmy Carr and Victoria Coren Mitchell join regular panellist Alan Davies to answer host Sandi Toksvig's questions on the theme of 'Oddballs'. During the course of the show, the panellists encounter some vegetarian goatsuckers. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the questions lead to points being awarded for the most interesting answers.(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Returning for the final time this season, a self-isolated Nish Kumar hosts Britain's most uncompromising topical satire, tackling all the troubling news from a world that's put its faith in Boris Johnson and Donald Trump. His similarly quarantined correspondents include Rachel Parris, Geoff Norcott and Ellie Taylor.(n)

programme.title: City of Tiny Lights (2016)
programme.desc: A hard-drinking private detective searches for a missing sex worker, leading to a dangerous world of religious fanaticism and political intrigue. London-set crime thriller from Dredd director Pete Travis, starring Riz Ahmed, Billie Piper and James Floyd.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Coronavirus: When Will We Be Safe? - Panorama
programme.desc: As politicians decide how and when to lift the lockdown, Justin Rowlatt discovers how science can help defeat the coronavirus. With access to key drug and vaccine trials, he reveals a race against time to help save lives, and asks when people are likely to be able to return to a normal life.(n)

programme.title: Terror in Paradise
programme.desc: Last Easter eight men triggered huge bombs in churches and tourist hotels across Sri Lanka, killing more than 270 people in one of the worst Islamist terror attacks since 9/11. Jane Corbin talks to survivors and those who came face to face with the killers. Drawing on hours of CCTV footage that track the bombers every move, the film pieces together how the attacks were carried out. Part of the This World strand.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson play video clips featuring a mysterious web that covered an entire street, and an Australian sea turned into a foam bath.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson plays clips featuring mice that invaded a farm in Australia and a swarm of locusts.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: The most unusual places in the UK.(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Joe Tracini and Meryl Fernandes enlist expert designers and builders to construct dream dens for lucky youngsters.(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the team continue to search for dangerous predators in Sri Lanka, as they hunt for the elusive mugger crocodile and the hook-nosed sea snake. They also explore the Yala National Park to catch a glimpse of the Sri Lankan leopard.(n)

programme.title: Astronauts: Do You Have What It Takes?
programme.desc: The last remaining candidates are put through a series of challenges to determine who will go through to the final stage. A survival test and a 30-metre abseil put their leadership abilities under scrutiny, before they face a series of gruelling interviews by two guest experts. Those who are chosen to continue head to Florida's Kennedy Space Centre, for a spacewalk training session in an underwater lab.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Kingdom (2015)
programme.desc: Documentary following a newborn monkey and its mother as they struggle to survive within the competitive social hierarchy of the Temple Troop, a group of simians who live in ancient ruins found deep in the jungles of Sri Lanka. Narrated by Tina Fey.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: British White cattle farmers Vivien, a retired GP, and David and Angela hope to help keep their rare breeds alive as they compete at the 104th Tendring Show in Essex. Tendring is their local show and it means a lot to them and the future of their beloved herds.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition continues, chefs Ruth Hansom, Alex Bond, Samira Effa and Josh Angus compete in the heats for the North East. Each chef prepares a starter and fish course celebrating iconic food from children's literature. This week, the chefs are specifically tasked with honouring the theme of realism in children's literature, as well as heralding books and authors from their region. Dishes include a Secret Garden-inspired starter of potatoes, alliums, egg and caviar, and a Railway Children-inspired creation comprising traditional North East delicacies.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Susan Calman presents as the chefs from the North East serve up their mains and desserts inspired by children's literature. One chef serves up a dish using oriental flavours inspired by the Chinese takeaway-loving title character in Skellig, while another creates a pork dish in honour of Smashing Saxons. The desserts see a pudding inspired by Jane Elson's A Room Full of Chocolate featuring a delicate chocolate egg, a second take on Skellig - this one using Newcastle Brown Ale ice-cream - and a dessert inspired by The Secret Garden, featuring Yorkshire rhubarb.(n)

programme.title: The Battle of the River Plate (1956)
programme.desc: British naval forces pursue German battleship the Admiral Graf Spee, finally blockading the vessel in Montevideo harbour, where its commanding officer is faced with an impossible choice. Second World War adventure from Powell and Pressburger, starring John Gregson, Anthony Quayle, Peter Finch, Bernard Lee and Jack Gwillim.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The ten remaining home sewers return to the nation's most famous sewing room for children's week, and are asked to make a classic smocked dress in the pattern challenge. The task requires the sewers to combine tricky techniques, including making their own bias binding, and a Peter Pan collar. Next, in the transformation challenge, the sewers let their imaginations run wild, turning sleeping bags into food shaped fancy dress. Finally, the contestants are asked to make a pair of dungarees for a little boy or girl.(n)

programme.title: The Eagle Has Landed (1976)
programme.desc: A Nazi secret agent plans to kidnap Winston Churchill while the prime minister spends a weekend in the Norfolk countryside, and plants covert operatives in the area ready for his arrival. Second World War adventure, adapted from Jack Higgins' novel, starring Michael Caine, Donald Sutherland, Robert Duvall, Jenny Agutter, Donald Pleasence, Anthony Quayle and Jean Marsh.(n)

programme.title: Racing Legends
programme.desc: Actor Patrick Stewart and former racing driver Stirling Moss travel to Florence in a classic Mercedes to retrace the route of Italy's most famous road race - the Mille Miglia - and discuss the possibility that a near-fatal crash may have been the best thing that ever happened to Moss. But how will Patrick cope when he drives a 1957 Formula One car at the scene of Stirling's historic British Grand Prix victory?(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's 2,500-mile railway tour of South-east Asia reaches its southern most point in Indonesia, with stop-offs in Java and Jakarta among the highlights. Michael uncovers Indonesia's colonial past as part of a Dutch empire and its rich history as a port and centre of the spice trade, negotiates the city's infamous traffic, takes a ride on the newly launched MRT metro and dines like a local, on Jakarta's famous street food.(n)

programme.title: Peter Sellers: A State of Comic Ecstasy
programme.desc: The comedy pioneer behind the Goon Show, Dr Strangelove and the Pink Panther series is explored in depth in this film surveying his meteoric rise to fame and troubled personal life. The film charts his formative years backstage as part of his parents' itinerant music hall revue group, his years of wartime service in India and Burma, through to his global superstardom. His two surviving children Sarah and Victoria recall the challenges of growing up alongside his mood swings, while his grandson Will explores the troubled legacy his grandfather left behind.(n)

programme.title: Talking Comedy
programme.desc: A look back at clips of the comedian and actor on some of the BBC's best-loved talk shows.(n)

programme.title: Chappaquiddick (2017)
programme.desc: In 1969, a car accident on Chappaquiddick Island, Massachusetts, involving Senator Ted Kennedy, claims the life of young campaign strategist Mary Jo Kopechne. Fact-based thriller, starring Jason Clarke, Kate Mara and Ed Helms.(n)

programme.title: The Lunchbox (2013)
programme.desc: A neglected wife hopes to get some appreciation from her husband by making him packed lunches containing love letters and sending them to his workplace. However, the packages are accidentally given to the wrong employee, who responds to the messages and becomes a close and trusted confidante. Romantic drama, starring Irrfan Khan and Nimrat Kaur. In Hindi and English.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Presenter Helen Skelton and expert designer Danny Clarke help an elderly gardener in Cumbria, whose pretty plot has been spoiled by building work.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley is on his home patch in north London as he explores the Lea Valley, paying a visit to one of the UK's biggest salad growers to see what impact coronavirus is having. On International Dawn Chorus Day, bird expert Adrian Thomas reveals what to listen out for, shepherdess Hannah Jackson talks about how she's coping with the lockdown, and Adam Henson goes flat out to get his spring barley in.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Rachel de Thame reviews the progress of her new kitchen garden, and begins planting out peas and sowing flower seeds. Nick Bailey shows how to make an easy cold frame for storing precious plants and seedlings, and Joe Swift meets Will Young, whose new-found love of gardening was key to his recovery from mental health problems.(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: How planting nettles on the island of Lewis creates the perfect habitat for a rare bird - the corncrake. Plus, Dougie investigates how the milk industry has been hit hard by lockdown, a young crofter sends a dispatch from the Fair Isle, and a look into the long-term impact of flooding.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: In Joppa, George creates a plant support for his sweet peas out of prunings, meanwhile Brian revels in the torrent of tulips at Scone.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The latest two highest-scoring North East chefs cook their six-course menus again to impress judges Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The arbitrators are joined by children's author Jacqueline Wilson, who has written over 100 novels since she started writing books at the age of nine. Presented by Susan Calman.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare dishes for when the temperature drops, such as oxtail soup with herby dumplings, a chestnut and mushroom pie and a new take on treacle sponge.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality renovations for a couple's home in Margate. The property was originally a B&B, and the transition to a home has not been entirely successful. It is poorly laid out, has too many rooms, and while big on floor space is lacking natural light. Laura sets out to return the building to its early 20th-century roots, whole Robert comes up with a radically different way of using the living space.(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: From the ancient temples of Angkor Wat in Cambodia to the Shaolin Temple in China, discover what people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth. In the opening episode, in Cambodia at Angkor Wat, a man who believes the temples are home to his ancestral spirits risks his life to save them from the jungle, while at the Shaolin Temple in China, a Buddhist warrior monk faces a test that could change the course of his life for ever.(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms revisits some of the great British war films that helped boost the nation's morale in the difficult years that followed the end of the Second World War. Among the silver screen gems highlighted are In Which We Serve, Reach for the Sky, Bridge on the River Kwai and The Dam Busters. The programme includes archive interviews with stars including Noel Coward, Richard Attenborough, David Lean, Alec Guinness, Kenneth More and John Mills, and Sylvia shares her memories of filming her own contribution to the genre, Ice Cold in Alex.(n)

programme.title: The Heroes of Telemark (1965)
programme.desc: German scientists in occupied Norway during the Second World War begin work on the manufacture of vital components for an atomic bomb, prompting the Norwegian resistance to team up with British forces on a campaign of sabotage to halt production. Second World War thriller, starring Kirk Douglas, Richard Harris, Anton Diffring, Roy Dotrice and Ulla Jacobsson.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a factory in Essex that produces 400,000 cereal bars a day, and follows the production process from the arrival of the ingredients to the eventual dispatch, discovering the importance of getting the perfect balance between honey and glucose. Cherry Healey travels to South Africa to help with the macadamia harvest, where a quarter of the world's supply of these nuts are farmed. Historian Ruth Goodman climbs a mountain in the Lake District to investigate the history of one of the oldest forms of snack bar - the Kendal Mint Cake.(n)

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels through Libya, visiting areas of the country devastated by recent fighting as well as one of the world's best preserved ancient Roman cities. He moves on to Tunisia, where he discovers a mountain fortress and meets a man keeping ancient Berber traditions alive, including making olive oil with the help of camels. Finally, he crosses the sea to Sicily, and discovers how the Mafia are attempting to take advantage of declining population numbers.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, founder of Crafter's Companion, is a new addition to the panel on the business ideas contest, joining returning dragons Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani in putting more would-be entrepreneurs' ideas to the test. Making their pitches this week are the creator of a brand of spiced rum, who gives the studio an exciting new look, a couple who have brought their four-legged friend with them to demonstrate a range of pet food, and the inventor of a special toilet gadget. Evan Davis presents.(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an isolation version of his topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests.(n)

programme.title: Dave
programme.desc: Sitcom starring American rapper and comedian Lil Dicky as Dave, a neurotic suburbanite who knows he is destined to be one of the world's biggest hip hop stars - but has a harder time convincing everyone else. While hanging around the music studio where his friend works, Dave meets fellow hanger-on GaTa, who attempts to fill him in on the complicated social dynamics of the rap world. He also promises to get him the chance to perform a guest verse for famous rapper YG, who is currently recording in the studio for one of his songs.(n)

programme.title: Dave
programme.desc: The would-be rapper is finally asked to give his first ever public performance, but the occasion is not exactly what he had always imagined. Elz hesitantly agrees to DJ, while flatmate Mike practices a new skincare routine on Dave.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: The housemates awaken to an unexpected crisis with no memory of how it happened, and find themselves put on trial by an international tribunal of vampires. Tilda Swinton heads up a star-studded guest cast, including Dave Bautista (Guardians of the Galaxy), Kristen Schaal (Flight of the Conchords), Danny Trejo (Machete), Wesley Snipes (Blade), Evan Rachel Wood (True Blood), and the stars of the original movie, Taika Waititi, Jemaine Clement and Jonny Brugh, reprising their roles.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: Nadja gives Jenna an introduction to life as a vampire. and trains her how to take her first victim. Nandor applies for American citizenship.(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting politicians and other guests to answer questions from viewers at home on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week.(n)

programme.title: Classic Cellists at the BBC
programme.desc: Julian Lloyd Webber examines the BBC archives from the 1950s to the present to celebrate the world of the cello through some of its greatest interpreters. From dazzling performances by legendary masters such as Paul Tortelier, Jacqueline du Pre and Mstislav Rostropovich to some of today's leading interpreters including Yo Yo Ma, Steven Isserlis and Mischa Maisky, the presenter offers a cellist's perspective on an extraordinary virtuoso tradition.(n)

programme.title: Tunes for Tyrants: Music & Power with Suzy Klein
programme.desc: Suzy Klein explores the use, abuse and manipulation of music in the Second World War - from swinging jazz to film soundtracks and from mushy ballads to madcap ballets. The war, she demonstrates, wasn't just a military fight but an ideological battle where both sides used music as a weapon to secure their vision for civilization.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Sybil Ruscoe host the edition first shown on August 10, featuring performances by Shakespears Sister, Jason Donovan, the Dogs d'Amour, Liza Minnelli, Aswad and Big Fun.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s 2
programme.desc: A celebration of disco music, including archive performances by George McCrae, Carl Douglas, Thelma Houston, Sylvester, Eruption, McFadden & Whitehead, Sister Sledge, Gloria Gaynor and the Jacksons.(n)

programme.title: Definitely Dusty
programme.desc: Documentary revisiting the career of Dusty Springfield, the renowned pop diva famous for classic songs such as You Don't Have to Say You Love Me and Son of a Preacher Man. Archive footage shot in both the UK and the US provides an illuminating insight into her glamorous but private personal life, and contributions from her protective inner circle of friends highlight the Dusty behind the panda eyes and blonde beehive. Includes interviews with Tom Jones, Elton John, Burt Bacharach, Lulu and Neil Tennant.(n)

programme.title: Dusty Springfield at the BBC
programme.desc: A tribute to the renowned London-born pop diva, featuring BBC archive footage of some of Dusty's most famous performances from 1961 to 1995. The programme also offers a chance to relive the singer's story, from her folk beginnings with the Springfields, through to her Motown homage years and her collaboration with Pet Shop Boys in the late 1980s. Also includes the star's duets with Tom Jones and Mel Torme.(n)

programme.title: People's History of Pop
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Danny Baker celebrates the British public's love affair with pop music from 1966 to 1976, including a rare and intimate view of David Bowie. A fan of Marc Bolan describes how the singer-songwriter's dad gave him a pair of the star's trainers when he went to his house, and a former teacher and pupil of Peckham Manor School are reunited, more than 40 years after they witnessed an unknown Bob Marley perform in their sports hall.(n)

programme.title: Jazz 625 Live: For One Night Only
programme.desc: A one-off revival of the iconic 1960s music show. Broadcasting live from Cheltenham during the Jazz Festival, the special show will feature a house band - led by pianist Robert Mitchell - with special guests including Gregory Porter, Joshua Redman, Jacqui Dankworth and Cleo Laine. Plus, an exclusive recorded performance from Rolling Stones drummer Charlie Watts in a jazz quartet with saxophonist Scott Hamilton. Presented by Andi Oliver.(n)

programme.title: Tunes for Tyrants: Music & Power with Suzy Klein
programme.desc: Suzy Klein explores the use, abuse and manipulation of music in the Second World War - from swinging jazz to film soundtracks and from mushy ballads to madcap ballets. The war, she demonstrates, wasn't just a military fight but an ideological battle where both sides used music as a weapon to secure their vision for civilization.(n)

programme.title: Primates
programme.desc: Chris Packham narrates a look at the complex social and family lives of the animal group, including leaf monkeys competing to babysit a bright orange infant and rally to defend it from a python, and gibbons learning daredevil treetop acrobatics with their playmates. In the Amazon, a female spider monkey elder leads its troop to aquatic plants brought within reach on raging floodwaters and shows them how they can feast on this unlikely bounty by using their remarkable tails as safety ropes.(n)

programme.title: How the Wild West Was Won with Ray Mears
programme.desc: The adventurer explores how the Great Plains - 500,000-square miles of flat, treeless grassland - became the setting for some of the Wild West's most dramatic events. Ray joins members of the Blackfeet Nation as they demonstrate bare-back riding skills before undertaking a ritual buffalo hunt and sacrifice. At a cattle auction in Dodge City, Kansas, he reveals stories about the railways and cow-towns before learning about the life of an iconic figure - the cowboy.(n)

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: Fredrik Nyman has to come up with a plan to save the factory, while Anna Hellevik sees an opportunity to help her father-in-law and starts scheming behind his back. Toril Torstensen gets help from an unexpected friend. In Norwegian and English.(n)

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: Ingrid plans a party for Fredrik's 50th birthday and breaks social rules to support a friend in trouble. Christian and the other divers at the Ocean Viking are taking risks every day at work, where things are getting increasingly dangerous. In Norwegian and English.(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: A selection of performances by some of the most popular all-girl groups to have appeared on the show, from the pop-soul vocal ensembles of the 1960s to the glamorous bands of the new millennium. Includes songs by the Supremes, Labelle, TLC, Spice Girls, the Pointer Sisters and B*Witched.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Sybil Ruscoe host the edition first shown on August 10, featuring performances by Shakespears Sister, Jason Donovan, the Dogs d'Amour, Liza Minnelli, Aswad and Big Fun.(n)

programme.title: The Jazz Ambassadors
programme.desc: In 1955 African-American Congressman Adam Clayton Powell Jr and trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie announced a new weapon to combat the Soviet Union - iconic jazz musicians and their racially integrated bands would cross the globe to counter propaganda about racism in America. Over the next decade, Louis Armstrong, Duke Ellington and Dave Brubeck toured the world in service of US Cold War interests. However, unfolding civil rights unrest back home forced them into a moral bind. This documentary recounts the story of how the US State Department unwittingly gave the Civil Rights movement a voice on the world stage when it needed one most.(n)

programme.title: Primates
programme.desc: Chris Packham narrates a look at the complex social and family lives of the animal group, including leaf monkeys competing to babysit a bright orange infant and rally to defend it from a python, and gibbons learning daredevil treetop acrobatics with their playmates. In the Amazon, a female spider monkey elder leads its troop to aquatic plants brought within reach on raging floodwaters and shows them how they can feast on this unlikely bounty by using their remarkable tails as safety ropes.(n)

programme.title: How the Wild West Was Won with Ray Mears
programme.desc: The adventurer explores how the Great Plains - 500,000-square miles of flat, treeless grassland - became the setting for some of the Wild West's most dramatic events. Ray joins members of the Blackfeet Nation as they demonstrate bare-back riding skills before undertaking a ritual buffalo hunt and sacrifice. At a cattle auction in Dodge City, Kansas, he reveals stories about the railways and cow-towns before learning about the life of an iconic figure - the cowboy.(n)

programme.title: BBC Young Musician
programme.desc: A flautist, clairinettist, oboist, recorder player and bassoonist compete for a place in the BBC Young Musician 2020 semi-finals, performing music by Vivaldi, Tchaikovsky, Messiaen and Peter Facer. Anna Lapwood is joined by principal flautist with the Royal Scottish National Orchestra, Katherine Bryan, to review each of the performers.(n)

programme.title: A Year in the Wild
programme.sub-title: Snowdonia - A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: The first of three films exploring life in Britain's national parks. Snowdonia in north Wales is a landscape formed by extinct volcanos, and is home to creatures including otters, hen harriers and peregrine falcons. The programme explores the area over the course of a year through the eyes of people who live and work there, including a warden, a farmer, a climber and a poet. Narrated by Hermione Norris.(n)

programme.title: David Stratton's Stories of Australian Cinema
programme.desc: How Australian cinema celebrates the endurance of outsiders, whether they are newcomers to a strange new land in They're a Weird Mob and Wake in Fright, or locals out of step with the mainstream in Evil Angels, Muriel's Wedding and Priscilla, Queen of the Desert.(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The programme celebrates its 800th episode, with filming taking place under lockdown, as Chris Lintott explores how some astronomers are managing to continue making observations - including controlling telescopes as far away as Hawaii from a laptop in their bedrooms in Belfast.(n)

programme.title: Retreat: Meditations from a Monastery
programme.desc: The second episode follows the a typical day in the life of the 21 monks residing in Pluscarden Abbey, a remote monastery on the edge of the Scottish Highlands in Moray. Given its isolated location, this Abbey is almost entirely self-sufficient, and the monks make their own clothes and grow their own crops, giving them little reason to have contact with the outside world.(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: The Ladybird Books Story - The Bugs That Got Britain Reading
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the much-loved children's books, which gained prominence during the 1950s, entertaining and informing youngsters as well as helping generations of pupils to read using their 'Key Words' literacy scheme. Narrated by Miles Jupp.(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Arthur, Buster and Brain are worried when Mr Ratburn isn't acting like himself. He's always pleased to see them, so what has made him change his tune?(n)

Then Captain Metimbers interrupts, she's locked out of her treasure chest and needs Phoebe to open it. programme.title: Dame Vera Lynn: We'll Meet Again
programme.desc: In a revised version of a documentary first shown in 2017 to mark the 100th birthday of the 'forces sweetheart', Katie Derham makes use of archive footage and family records to tell the story of how Vera Lynn became one of the UK's most well-known musical stars. Vera marked her centenary by reminiscing about her early days in showbusiness, with help from her daughter Virginia. She recalls starting out as Vera Welch in the 1920s, first appearing on stage at the tender age of seven, before charming the nation's servicemen during the Second World War. She also remembers becoming the first British artist to achieve a number one in America in the 1950s, and a similar feat in 2009, when she became the oldest living singer to reach the same position in the UK charts. Along the way, Second World War veterans and entertainment industry figures - including Paul McCartney, Barry Humphries and Miriam Margolyes - pay tribute to the power of her voice.(n)

programme.title: Our Finest Hours
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth reflects on how families, friends and neighbours have pulled together during the coronavirus lockdown, just as they did 80 years ago during the Second World War. As the crisis has worsened, the support for each other - and especially for NHS frontline staff - has strengthened. Different generations have all done their bit to keep themselves and those around then safe and to look after the most vulnerable members of the community.(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood takes a journey through the world of horticulture, getting inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters. programme.title: VE Day 75: The Announcement of Victory
programme.desc: On May 8 1945, Winston Churchill broadcast the announcement that the war in Europe was over. To mark the 75th anniversary of this historic and unforgettable event, Sophie Raworth presents a special tribute to the Second World War generation. Through stirring military music, moving personal testimony and readings from VIPS and celebrities, all those who fought and contributed to the war effort are remembered and celebrated.(n)

programme.title: VE Day 75: The People's Celebration
programme.desc: To mark the 75th anniversary of VE Day, the BBC and the Royal British Legion honour and celebrate the Second World War generation in a programme featuring recollections from some those who remember this historic day. Popular songs of the era are performed by stars including Katherine Jenkins, Adrian Lester, Anton Du Beke, Beverley Knight, Helen George, Sharon D Clarke, Shane Richie and Emma Barton. In a fitting finale at 9.00, the nation is asked to unite for a very special rendition of We'll Meet Again, as a gesture of thanks to the veterans and in memory of lost loved ones. Presented by Sophie Raworth.(n)

programme.title: VE Day: Remembering Victory
programme.desc: May 8, 2015 marked 70 years since Winston Churchill announced the Second World War in Europe was over, prompting Britain to throw the biggest street party the country has ever seen. David Attenborough, Bruce Forsyth, Miriam Margolyes and June Whitfield are just a few of the well-known figures sharing their memories of the end of the conflict. Narrated by Julie Walters, with contributions from others including Johnny Ball, Honor Blackman, Jilly Cooper, John Craven, Cleo Laine, Kenny Lynch, Michael Parkinson, Leslie Phillips, Anne Reid, Esther Rantzen, Patrick Stewart and Una Stubbs. First shown in 2015.(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At the recycling centre in Woking, upcycling aficionado EJ Osborne unearths three hidden gems - an unwanted windbreaker, some old workshop cast-offs and a child's chair.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in York, helping a couple who have spent two years painstakingly renovating their terraced home. With a very, very small garden, full of crazy paving and building materials, Charlie and the boys compete to give it a £5,000k facelift, turning it into a luxurious haven to relax and entertain in. Along the way, they have a host of ideas and tips for making the most of small spaces and shady corners.(n)

programme.title: Penguins - Spy in the Huddle
programme.desc: The second part of the documentary sees the chicks hatching in Antarctica, the Falklands and Peru, leading to delightful scenes as the new parents help their young take their first few unsteady steps. All three colonies share one thing in common, however - protecting their babies from predators eager to get hold of the new arrivals.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is on her home patch in West Yorkshire, visiting Harewood House to learn more about its conservation projects and dropping in to her local horse and donkey sanctuary, Hope Pastures. Olympic medal-winning triathletes Jonny and Alistair Brownlee demonstrate how to keep fit under lockdown, Tom Heap discovers how school closures have hit vital outdoor education, and Adam Henson steps in to help a nanny goat in distress.(n)

programme.desc: An update from the Prime Minister on the coronavirus epidemic.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: In a special edition, Fiona Bruce marks the 75th anniversary of VE Day, looking back at objects featured on the programme that gave personal insights into the momentous events of the Second World War. There is first-hand testimony from those who were present at VE Day, including the Roadshow's own Henry Sandon, while Fiona also delves into the BBC archives to discover the challenges of reporting the announcement and the celebrations across the UK and abroad.(n)

programme.title: Captain Tom: We Salute You
programme.desc: The incredible story of the Second World War veteran Captain Tom Moore. To mark his 100th birthday, Captain Tom Moore decided to try and raise £1,000 for the NHS to help support them during the coronavirus outbreak, but four weeks later, he had raised more than £32 million, had a number one single and become a global sensation.(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: Villanelle receives good news about her forthcoming promotion, but can't shake the feeling that there is still something missing from her life, while Dasha seeks a solution to the assassin's erratic behaviour. Eve tries to patch up things with Niko on her birthday.(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.sub-title: True North: Crossmaglen - Field of Dreams
programme.desc: During the Troubles, Crossmaglen was seen by many as a place apart, but a group of children managed to form one of the most formidable football teams in GAA club history. This documentary follows Crossmaglen Rangers GAC over the course of two seasons as joint managers Oisin McConville and John McEntee - both former All-Ireland winners with the club - attempt to guide the next generation to victory.(n)

programme.title: The Thomas Crown Affair (1999)
programme.desc: A thrill-seeking billionaire develops a taste for cat burglary, and lifts a priceless Monet from a New York gallery in broad daylight. However, he later meets his match in a cunning female insurance investigator and the pair embark on a whirlwind romance - but their conflicting careers threaten their relationship. Crime adventure remake, with Pierce Brosnan, Rene Russo, Denis Leary and Ben Gazzara.(n)

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Science and Technology select committee session on the coronavirus pandemic with evidence from national statistician Professor Sir Ian Diamond, infectious disease modeller Professor John Edmunds, chair of the department for international health at John Hopkins University, Professor David Peters and Winton Professor of the Public Understanding of Risk at Cambridge University, Professor Sir David Spiegelhalter, from Thursday 7 May.(n)

programme.desc: The ad hoc committee sitting in the Northern Ireland Assembly focusing on the coronavirus pandemic including a statement from the Economy Minister, Diane Dodds, from earlier today.(n)

programme.desc: The first Duke of Wellington - victor at the Battle of Waterloo - was a military hero who served twice as Prime Minister. Carolyn Quinn meets the current Duke for a tour of the family's London home, Apsley House.(n)

programme.title: Captain Tom's War
programme.desc: Just a week after his 100th birthday celebrations, NHS fundraiser extraordinaire and national treasure Captain Tom Moore marks another upcoming milestone in this documentary - 75 years since VJ Day and the end of the Second World War later this summer. He looks back on his time in the brutal Burma campaign and honours the soldiers who fought in what has since become known - with so much focus on the European battlefront and on today's VE Day commemoration - as the 'forgotten war'.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin

Louisa calls Martin after Danny collapses - but the ambulance is delayed and emergency surgery must be performed to save his life.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A middle-aged Oxford graduate is found murdered, setting in motion an investigation that leads Lewis and Hathaway to one of the university's most senior dons, whose respectable exterior conceals a strange past. Starring Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox, Anna Massey, Clare Holman and Sian Thomas.(n)

Laurence Fox and Rebecca Front co-star alongside Kevin Whately.(n)

programme.desc: During a music masterclass led by a renowned pianist, a shy prodigy suffers a panic attack and runs off to a nearby river. But when Barnaby arrives, there is no sign of the victim - and he is struck by similarities to another mysterious case that happened 20 years ago at the same spot. programme.title: Inspector Morse

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The killing of a postman, whose doorstep deliveries often included more than just letters, rocks the idyllic rural hamlet of Goodman's Land and sends Barnaby on the trail of a murderer with vengeance in mind - if Sgt Troy can keep his eyes off the attractive village policewoman long enough to follow the clues. programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Ann Smith, a 53-year-old grandmother from Watford, is haunted by unanswered questions about her past. As a baby, Ann was adopted by George and Jean Smith, but despite growing up in a loving family, Ann was always aware that she was different, and began the search for her birth mother. Meanwhile, 73-year-old Derek Berg searches for his brother who was given up by his parents.
