programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: An exhausted Yasmeen sits in her police cell while the police search No 6 for evidence, unaware that they are being filmed on Geoff's CCTV. Alya returns from Spain and heads straight to the police station, Gary leaps to Kelly's defence when Dev blames her for heaping so much trouble on Asha, and Adam and Sarah return from their honeymoon.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Imran watches on as a bewildered and upset Yasmeen is charged, while Paul puts pressure on Billy to chuck Kelly out of the flat after discovering her role in sharing Asha's video. Adam learns that Ali has left the area after pleading guilty to assaulting Gary, and David wakes with a start and realises with horror that he's missed Max's speech.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Will is horrified to learn from Malone that he is planning on arresting Billy and Cain himself, while Dan's adamant he doesn't need any help from Amelia, but when he falls out of his wheelchair it's clear he's not coping. Belle shares a drink at the Woolpack with Jamie, but they're soon joined by a knowing Andrea who tells Jamie she and him need to take a holiday with one another.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Will hasn't returned home and Harriet is in the dark over what is going on, while an excited Rishi decides he wants to go camping for his 70th birthday. Priya is disappointed that she has to attend her father's party over spending time with Al, and Belle frets at the thought of Jamie going on holiday with Andrea.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Harriet is angry as Will continues to try to make excuses, while matters between Arthur and Archie get out of hand at Rishi's party, and Vanessa continues with her plans. Wendy is enthusiastic when Victoria leaves her alone with Harry for a brief period, and Dan reveals his fall yesterday has put him back further.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The master of a prestigious college asks Morse to investigate the disappearance of one of his colleagues. The subsequent discovery of a dismembered torso soon has everyone fearing the worst - until the missing man turns up alive and well. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, James Grout, Beatie Edney, Sian Thomas and Barry Foster.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Matron (1972)
programme.desc: A wily thief gathers a band of crooks for a plot to break into a maternity hospital and steal the supply of contraceptive pills. One of the gang gets the lie of the land by disguising himself as a nurse, but the hypochondriac head doctor and formidable matron prove constant obstacles to the heist. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams and Hattie Jacques.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Milton Cross, a feudal estate with a church, farms, houses and acres of land, depend on lord of the manor Edward Milton for their livelihoods. When local woman Sonia Woodley is stabbed to death in the churchyard two years after the death of her husband, bespoke tailor Gerald, Barnaby and Jones investigate, and the village begins to reveal its secrets. Detective drama, guest starring James Wilby, Gwyneth Strong and Karl Davies (formerly Emmerdale's Robert Sugden), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham.(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: As Cassie and Sunny continue their investigation into the cold-case murder, the net tightens around the people whose names were found in the victim's diary. Sir Phillip Cross makes a difficult decision to salvage what is left of his reputation while at the same time protecting his family, Father Robert comes clean to the police and Ray returns home to find Lizzie gone. Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar, with Trevor Eve, Bernard Hill, Tom Courtenay, Ruth Sheen and Brian Bovell.(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Father Robert's revelations prove to have lasting repercussions, especially for Caroline when she goes into premature labour. Bella and Josh deliberate over the best way to deal with their father, the search for Lizzie continues and Maureen is finally able to lay Jimmy to rest. Conclusion of the crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar, with Bernard Hill, Tamzin Malleson, Zoe Telford, Trevor Eve, Tom Courtenay and Tom Austen.(n)

programme.title: Tina & Bobby
programme.desc: Drama about England and West Ham star Bobby Moore's life with his childhood sweetheart Tina Dean, detailing how the pair rose to the dizzy heights of superstardom. In the opening instalment, the 19-year-old insurance office typist agrees to marry the footballer, little knowing that their marriage will be defined by history. Their lives change markedly when the talented player becomes England captain aged just 23. However, when he is diagnosed with cancer Tina proves Bobby's rock as he fights his way back to fitness in a bid to compete in the World Cup of 1966. Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan star.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve asks Alec to drop the hostilities as it's upsetting Vicky. Bet is horrified when Alec asks Nick to lie to Vicky about the terms of the will.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin is stunned to receive a message from his estranged father. Deirdre is unnerved by Cropper's advances towards her. Alec lies to Steve about a clause in the Ardens' wil.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dave spends Christmas night at Home Farm with Kim. Butch and Sam are unwittingly coerced into helping Albert with a robbery. Frank is rushed back to hospital in an ambulance.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: No-nonsense teacher Jim Osgood uses his experience of the Territorial Army to impose strict discipline at Ashfordly School, but he goes too far with schoolboy troublemaker Gary Bell and puts him in hospital. Determined to get revenge, the youngster breaks into Osgood's office and steals his gun, leaving Mason with little time to avert a tragedy. Joe McFadden and Lisa Kay star.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: A Season of Heavenly Gifts - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop befriends a pilot he rescues from his crashed plane, but suspects he is being drawn into a smuggling racket when the family is invited to a celebratory champagne night on the town. Drama, starring David Jason.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo are seeking an additional female flatmate, so Robin has to get in touch with his feminine side. The first episode of the 1970s comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox and Sally Thomsett.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An accountant and his family crash into a tractor after being chased down a country lane by another car. Mason is suspicious of them and investigates the incident, discovering they are on the run from a gangster who has arrived in Aidensfield determined to retrieve a set of incriminating documents at any cost. Meanwhile, David buys an old Jeep with a surprising military history. Starring Joe McFadden.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is furious when he finds out what Butch and Sam have been up to. Tina is out walking when she is grabbed by a mystery man. Chris and Kim have another argument about Frank.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dave's attempt to make friends with Nick ends in a fight. Dave gives Kim an ultimatum about their future together. Albert returns to the Dingles to face the music with Zak.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vicky asks Alec to stop being so nasty. Deirdre tells Mike that she finds Cropper too over-friendly. Derek constructs a trap in his garden to catch the gnome thief.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An accountant and his family crash into a tractor after being chased down a country lane by another car. Mason is suspicious of them and investigates the incident, discovering they are on the run from a gangster who has arrived in Aidensfield determined to retrieve a set of incriminating documents at any cost. Meanwhile, David buys an old Jeep with a surprising military history. Starring Joe McFadden.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Pauline is all set to achieve her ambition when she is given the chance to join the Portwenn lifeboat crew. However, her joy is short-lived when she discovers the chauvinistic tendencies of her shipmates. Martin is accused of murder by the husband of a woman who died in his care. The irate man resorts to sabotaging Aunt Joan's crops in retaliation but an unexpected twist reveals the true motivation for his ire.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor is surprised when his parents visit out of the blue, but they bring unwelcome tidings for all, especially Aunt Joan, who may be driven from her home. School secretary Maureen receives better news when what she thought was her thyroid problem turns out to be a pregnancy. Louisa calls Martin after Danny collapses - but the ambulance is delayed and emergency surgery must be performed to save his life.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A middle-aged Oxford graduate is found murdered, setting in motion an investigation that leads Lewis and Hathaway to one of the university's most senior dons, whose respectable exterior conceals a strange past. Before long a second death occurs, and it becomes apparent that both victims shared a secret - and that someone is out for revenge. Starring Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox, Anna Massey, Clare Holman and Sian Thomas.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The members of an Oxford old boys' cricket team gather for their annual match, unaware that death is waiting at the crease to bowl one of them out for good. Morse sets out to find the culprit - helped as ever by the faithful Lewis, who demonstrates unexpected skill on the pitch. John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Sharon Maughan star in an episode scripted by Oscar-winning director Anthony Minghella.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Cheap Flat
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: In a bid to find true love, Rigsby puts a personal advertisement in the local paper and receives an unexpected reply. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington star.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Hemel Hempstead, Hertfordshire, where they meet deaf couple Asif Iqbal and wife Sabina. The green-fingered experts attempt to turn a boring lawn and crumbling patio into a Bollywood dream garden with an outdoor kitchen for Asif, Sabina and their family.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Nurse Maggie Simkiss cares for end of life patients and treats their emotional well-being as well as their direct medical needs. To say thank you for her hard work, Alan Titchmarsh and his team of green-fingered experts arrive at her Hull home and help transform the unkempt garden into a grown-up Mediterranean hideaway.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen faces the consequences of her red mist, as the paramedics attend to Geoff. She arrives at the police station to find Imran waiting for her, having taken a call from Ryan, while in the Rovers, the police question the regulars about Geoff and Yasmeen's relationship.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: An exhausted Yasmeen sits in her police cell while the police search No 6 for evidence, unaware that they are being filmed on Geoff's CCTV. Alya returns from Spain and heads straight to the police station, Gary leaps to Kelly's defence when Dev blames her for heaping so much trouble on Asha, and Adam and Sarah return from their honeymoon.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Imran watches on as a bewildered and upset Yasmeen is charged, while Paul puts pressure on Billy to chuck Kelly out of the flat after discovering her role in sharing Asha's video. Adam learns that Ali has left the area after pleading guilty to assaulting Gary, and David wakes with a start and realises with horror that he's missed Max's speech.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Loving (1970)
programme.desc: A couple who run a dating agency find themselves inundated with customers, and end up arranging a selection of very unlikely matches - while also contending with their own marital difficulties. Comedy, starring Sid James, Hattie Jacques, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Terry Scott.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The inspector and his sidekick DS Hathaway investigate the murder of a talented maths student. The prime suspect is the victim's ex-boyfriend, who is convinced his uncle has set him up, but old case papers belonging to Morse reveal the boy's family is hiding a dark secret. Laurence Fox and Rebecca Front co-star alongside Kevin Whately.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: During a music masterclass led by a renowned pianist, a shy prodigy suffers a panic attack and runs off to a nearby river. To her horror, she witnesses a woman drowning and a baby left on the riverbank. But when Barnaby arrives, there is no sign of the victim - and he is struck by similarities to another mysterious case that happened 20 years ago at the same spot. Guest starring veteran actor James Fox and Coronation Street's Ian Puleston-Davies (Owen Armstrong), alongside former Corrie star Richard Fleeshman (Craig Harris).(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Crime drama, starring John Thaw as the ale-swilling, crossword-loving detective with a passion for opera and an eye for murder, assisted by the ever-game Lewis (Kevin Whately) as they solve some of Oxford's most complex mysteries.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The killing of a postman, whose doorstep deliveries often included more than just letters, rocks the idyllic rural hamlet of Goodman's Land and sends Barnaby on the trail of a murderer with vengeance in mind - if Sgt Troy can keep his eyes off the attractive village policewoman long enough to follow the clues. Murder mystery, guest starring Robert Glenister, with John Nettles, Daniel Casey and Gillian Kearney.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A middle-aged Oxford graduate is found murdered, setting in motion an investigation that leads Lewis and Hathaway to one of the university's most senior dons, whose respectable exterior conceals a strange past. Before long a second death occurs, and it becomes apparent that both victims shared a secret - and that someone is out for revenge. Starring Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox, Anna Massey, Clare Holman and Sian Thomas.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Western Star
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Ann Smith, a 53-year-old grandmother from Watford, is haunted by unanswered questions about her past. As a baby, Ann was adopted by George and Jean Smith, but despite growing up in a loving family, Ann was always aware that she was different, and began the search for her birth mother. Meanwhile, 73-year-old Derek Berg searches for his brother who was given up by his parents. His mother Freda had an affair with an Allied soldier, and like many other illegitimate wartime babies, Derek's long lost sibling was put up for adoption.(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.desc: New tenant Ambrose bamboozles Rigsby by claiming to be a mystic, and soon has the other lodgers under a strange spell. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington star.(n)

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.sub-title: It Could be Practically Anywhere on the Island
programme.desc: A trip to Malta sees Harry called on to investigate the abduction of a woman's pet poodle. Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter star.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The coroner's investigation into the death of an air hostess opens a can of worms involving a crooked narcotics officer involved in a drug smuggling operation.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A woman claims that a supposedly dead criminal is still alive, and intends to kill her to cover his tracks. Theo dismisses her story - until she dies under suspicious circumstances.(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A valuable gem is stolen from the Countess of Morcar, leading the famous detective into a bizarre investigation involving a recovered Christmas goose. Mystery, starring Jeremy Brett, David Burke and Ken Campbell.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: When Dave's relief manager lets him down at short notice, he asks Terry to step into the breach and run the Winchester Club for him. However, concerns mount for both Terry and Arthur when the landlord fails to return as planned - especially as the club's licence is due for imminent renewal. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: Columbo and the Murder of a Rock Star (1991)
programme.desc: The crafty detective pits his wits against a brilliant defence lawyer who has never lost a case. The attorney has murdered his rock star girlfriend to prevent her exposing his unethical courtroom tactics, and then framed her lover for the crime. Drama, starring Peter Falk, Little Richard, Dabney Coleman, Shera Danese, Cheryl Paris and Julian Stone.(n)

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The super-sleuth visits a village to investigate a number of mysterious deaths, and tries to prove they have a logical rather than a supernatural explanation. Feature-length drama, starring Jeremy Brett, with Roy Marsden and Keith Barron.(n)

programme.title: The Sea Wolves (1980)
programme.desc: The German Navy use three Goa-based vessels to attack Allied shipping, and their British counterparts cannot retaliate as they are moored in neutral waters. A group of retired officers living in India unofficially returns to duty to deal with the threat. Fact-based Second World War adventure, starring Gregory Peck, Roger Moore, David Niven, Trevor Howard, Barbara Kellerman and Patrick Macnee.(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news.(n)

Fact-based Second World War adventure, starring Gregory Peck, Roger Moore, David Niven, Trevor Howard, Barbara Kellerman and Patrick Macnee.(n)

programme.title: Battle of Britain (1969)
programme.desc: In 1940, the Luftwaffe launches a campaign of aerial attacks against Britain to gain control of the skies ahead of a planned land invasion. Under the leadership of Air Chief Marshal Hugh Dowding, the RAF mounts a determined defence over the summer and autumn, despite being heavily outnumbered and forced to match inexperienced pilots against German veterans. Second World War drama, starring Laurence Olivier, Michael Caine, Robert Shaw, Christopher Plummer, Harry Andrews, Trevor Howard and Curt Jurgens.(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news.(n)

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.sub-title: The Big Fish Off: Best Ever Eps
programme.desc: Game show in which celebrities face a series of angling challenges. Actor Scott Maslen takes on record-breaking Essex cricketer Graham Napier, who is teamed up with his old school friend Ali Hamidi, as they fish the waters of Suffolk and Norfolk, where carp, bream and tench are on the agenda.(n)

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Fisherman Ali Hamidi is joined by Tom Dove and Neil Spooner to catch buffalo carp in Texas.(n)

programme.title: 8 Mile (2002)
programme.desc: A white rapper strives to make a name for himself on the music scene, but his deprived home life on a Detroit trailer park and romantic complications put a damper on his plans as he struggles to win over the crowds and earn the respect of his peers. Drama, starring Eminem, Kim Basinger, Mekhi Phifer and Brittany Murphy.(n)

programme.title: Jeremiah Johnson (1972)

Western, starring Robert Redford, Stefan Gierasch and Will Geer.(n)

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney travel to Dorset with England international angler Matt Godfrey, where they target a 20lb carp on an overnight challenge, before trying their hand at sea fishing for wrasse at Portland Bill, and finally trying to catch a 5lb chub at Throop Fishery.(n)

programme.title: Scarface (1983)
programme.desc: Small-time crook Tony Montana emigrates from Cuba to the US, determined to become rich and powerful. Admitted into the inner circle of a Miami drug lord, he ultimately takes over his boss's empire and even his wife. However, his paranoia and cocaine addiction begin to spiral out of control, while his enemies circle, waiting for the chance to bring him down. Gangster thriller remake, with Al Pacino, Michelle Pfeiffer and Robert Loggia.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan uses a criminal's abandoned dog as bait to lure his remorseful owner back into the country from France. Alfred Marks, Brian Glover and Brian Wilde guest star, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Monster Carp

programme.title: Brothers in Football
programme.desc: Following south London amateur football team Corinthian-Casuals as they travel to Sao Paulo in Brazil to fulfil a tour abandoned 100 years before on the eve of the First World War, facing two-time FIFA Club World Cup-winners SC Corinthians Paulista. The documentary also commemorates the original Corinthian FC, a club credited with popularising the sport around the world.(n)

programme.title: How the West Was Won (1962)
programme.desc: Three generations of a pioneering family experience many ordeals and adventures in their endeavour to build a new life for themselves on the American frontier in the 19th century. Over the years, the family experiences the key events of the nation's history, including the gold rush, the Civil War, and the building of the railroads. Epic Western, starring James Stewart, John Wayne, Gregory Peck, Henry Fonda, Debbie Reynolds, Lee J Cobb and Richard Widmark.(n)

programme.title: High Plains Drifter (1973)
programme.desc: A mysterious stranger is hired to defend a remote frontier town from three outlaws who are making people's lives a misery. Although he rids the community of the troublemakers, the residents' lives take a turn for the worse as their saviour seizes control of the town and seeks to punish them for their past sins. Western, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood, alongside Verna Bloom and Marianna Hill.(n)

programme.sub-title: It Could be Practically Anywhere on the Island
programme.desc: A trip to Malta sees Harry called on to investigate the abduction of a woman's pet poodle. Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter star.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The coroner's investigation into the death of an air hostess opens a can of worms involving a crooked narcotics officer involved in a drug smuggling operation.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A woman claims that a supposedly dead criminal is still alive, and intends to kill her to cover his tracks. Theo dismisses her story - until she dies under suspicious circumstances.(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A valuable gem is stolen from the Countess of Morcar, leading the famous detective into a bizarre investigation involving a recovered Christmas goose. Mystery, starring Jeremy Brett, David Burke and Ken Campbell.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: When Dave's relief manager lets him down at short notice, he asks Terry to step into the breach and run the Winchester Club for him. However, concerns mount for both Terry and Arthur when the landlord fails to return as planned - especially as the club's licence is due for imminent renewal. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: Columbo and the Murder of a Rock Star (1991)
programme.desc: The crafty detective pits his wits against a brilliant defence lawyer who has never lost a case. The attorney has murdered his rock star girlfriend to prevent her exposing his unethical courtroom tactics, and then framed her lover for the crime. Drama, starring Peter Falk, Little Richard, Dabney Coleman, Shera Danese, Cheryl Paris and Julian Stone.(n)

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The super-sleuth visits a village to investigate a number of mysterious deaths, and tries to prove they have a logical rather than a supernatural explanation. Feature-length drama, starring Jeremy Brett, with Roy Marsden and Keith Barron.(n)

programme.title: The Sea Wolves (1980)
programme.desc: The German Navy use three Goa-based vessels to attack Allied shipping, and their British counterparts cannot retaliate as they are moored in neutral waters. A group of retired officers living in India unofficially returns to duty to deal with the threat. Fact-based Second World War adventure, starring Gregory Peck, Roger Moore, David Niven, Trevor Howard, Barbara Kellerman and Patrick Macnee.(n)

programme.title: Battle of Britain (1969)
programme.desc: In 1940, the Luftwaffe launches a campaign of aerial attacks against Britain to gain control of the skies ahead of a planned land invasion. Under the leadership of Air Chief Marshal Hugh Dowding, the RAF mounts a determined defence over the summer and autumn, despite being heavily outnumbered and forced to match inexperienced pilots against German veterans. Second World War drama, starring Laurence Olivier, Michael Caine, Robert Shaw, Christopher Plummer, Harry Andrews, Trevor Howard and Curt Jurgens.(n)

programme.title: VE Day: The Lost Films
programme.desc: The story of VE Day in 1945, told through the eyewitness accounts and cine films of ordinary people. Amateur film-makers recorded remarkable scenes, with some of it in glorious colour. Now, rarely seen footage, captured by amateurs and stored away for decades, charts the extraordinary events of May 8 when the entire nation took to the streets. Shots include events in the heart of the madness in central London, village life in Chapeltown on the outskirts of Sheffield and reaction in , Tyne and Wear.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Cameras capture a route through Spain, beginning near Leon, part of the famous north Spanish Pilgrimage, in the Picos de Europa mountains. The railway then runs through the Basque country before the famous Guggenheim Museum greets travellers in Bilbao. In Galicia, the gorges, coastline and sea bridges are a sight for sore eyes, and a speciality cider tour refreshes the train's guests. The journey ends in Ferrol, a spectacular harbour that has been a major shipbuilding centre for most of its history and has been a key city in many wars. Narrated by Bill Nighy.(n)

programme.title: Around the World by Train with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: The last leg of Tony's round the world adventure begins deep in the Arctic Circle. His first stop is at a Polar Park working with wolves on a fascinating but brave project. Tony then rides the Arctic Circle Train south to the town of Kiruna, on the edge of Swedish Lapland, which is undergoing a very unique transformation as it's being moved building by building to a whole new area. Tony also gets to see behind the scenes at the famous Ice Hotel, a structure which is rebuilt every year from ice taken from the nearby Torne river and ends his journey at Miniature Wonderland - the world's largest model railway.(n)

Action thriller, starring Harrison Ford, Gary Oldman, Glenn Close, Dean Stockwell and William H Macy.(n)

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: Jane McDonald travels along the Danube on one of the most luxurious ships of its type, as she makes a foray into the realms of the river cruise Beginning in Budapest, Jane heads to the Szechenyi Baths, where she indulges in a game of chess. Afterwards, a visit to the Second World War memorial Shoes on the Danube Bank, leaves her in tears. Heading onwards to Vienna, the presenter is all set to meet a real-life princess. Then, after a waltz and a wurst, Jane makes her way to her final stop of Passau, southern Bavaria, to view its charming architecture.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Royal Wardrobe
programme.desc: The designers who create outfits for royal occasions, including wedding dresses. Featuring a look at some of the most memorable dresses and interviews with their creators. Plus, a look at outfits that were designed for Princess Diana but never worn and the reason the Queen always stands out in a crowd.(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Speeches: In Triumph & Tragedy
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at a selection of Elizabeth II's most famous speeches and the events that inspired them, including the deaths of Diana, Princess of Wales and the Queen Mother, Britain entering the first Gulf War, and the coronavirus pandemic. Royal experts and insiders provide the context of what has led to these addresses to the nation, revealing what prompted the monarch to take to the airwaves to reassure her subjects, how she managed it, and whether she captured the mood of the public.(n)

programme.title: Funny Women
programme.desc: A celebration of the careers of Dawn French and Jennifer Saunders. The duo started out in the famous Comic Strip troupe of the early '80s before landing their own show on the BBC, which ran for 20 years. Brilliant together, they also enjoyed stunning solo careers, and made two of the biggest hit sitcoms of the 1990s - Absolutely Fabulous and The Vicar of Dibley. The programme features the thoughts of friends and co-stars, including Jools Holland, Sarah Hadland, Helen Lederer and Christopher Biggins.(n)

programme.title: Funny Women
programme.sub-title: The Outrageous Bette Midler: By Her Friends
programme.desc: Celebrity friends and fans, including James Dreyfus, Wayne Sleep, Christopher Biggins, Michelle Visage and Melissa Manchester, pay homage to the actress and singer. This documentary traces Bette Midler's life from her childhood in Hawaii, her residency at a gay New York bathhouse accompanied on piano by a young Barry Manilow, through an astonishing Hollywood career to a recent record-breaking run on Broadway in Hello Dolly. The life of this pint-sized powerhouse is illustrated with rarely seen archive.(n)

Meanwhile, Jo is away on business so Steve will be left as a single parent - how will he cope given he has never even worked a washing machine?(n)

programme.desc: A woman is happy to be called up for service during the Second World War, but disappointed to learn she will be working in a factory making aircraft parts. However, the new job brings romance in the shape of a handsome airman and a close circle of friends in her co-workers, who all rally round when tragedy strikes. Drama, starring Patricia Roc, Eric Portman, Gordon Jackson and Basil Radford.(n)

programme.title: Two Thousand Women (1944)
programme.desc: Female prisoners struggle to cope with living in a German internment camp, but find common cause in making life difficult for their captors. When three British airmen are shot down over the prison, they keep them hidden from the Germans while plotting a way to smuggle them out safely. Second World War comedy, with Phyllis Calvert, Flora Robson, Patricia Roc and Renee Houston.(n)

programme.title: Waterloo Road (1944)
programme.desc: A soldier serving in the Second World War discovers his wife has betrayed him by having an affair with a petty crook, so he resolves to settle matters and deserts from the army. He returns to a Blitz-ravaged London to confront his rival, but his quest for retribution is interrupted by an air raid. Drama, starring John Mills, Joy Shelton, Alastair Sim and Stewart Granger.(n)

programme.title: The Land Girls (1997)
programme.desc: Three Land Army agricultural volunteers from very different social backgrounds are sent to work on a Dorset farm. Despite their differences, the three women become close friends, and one falls in love with the farmer's son. Wartime drama, with Catherine McCormack, Anna Friel, Rachel Weisz, Tom Georgeson and Steven Mackintosh.(n)

programme.title: Jason Bourne (2016)
programme.desc: Former government operative Jason Bourne has been in hiding for 12 years after exposing a sinister CIA operation. However, he once again finds himself being hunted down when a former CIA operative gone rogue acquires top secret information - including details of Bourne's own father's involvement in a clandestine scheme. Action thriller, starring Matt Damon, Tommy Lee Jones, Alicia Vikander and Julia Stiles.(n)

programme.title: Daybreakers (2009)
programme.desc: In the near future, vampires have taken over the world, with the few remaining humans farmed as a food source. An undead scientist tries to develop synthetic blood, only to run into a revolutionary group claiming to have discovered a cure for vampirism. Thriller, starring Ethan Hawke, Willem Dafoe and Sam Neill.(n)

programme.title: Machete Kills (2013)

A billionaire arms dealer intends to start a nuclear war using a space-based weapon of mass destruction, but defeating his schemes involves a deadly encounter with a murderous master of disguise. Action thriller sequel, starring Danny Trejo and Mel Gibson.(n)

programme.title: Those Glory, Glory Days (1983)
programme.desc: A journalist with a passion for football has a chance meeting with her soccer idol Danny Blanchflower, triggering memories of 1961, and her teenage obsession with Tottenham Hotspur as well as her close friendship with three other equally football-obsessed girls. Drama, starring Zoe Nathenson, Julia McKenzie, Sara Sugarman, Cathy Murphy and Blanchflower as himself.(n)

programme.title: The Choir (2014)
programme.desc: A troubled young orphan is sent to an elite choral academy following the death of his single mother. He rebels against its rules, but the school's choirmaster is determined to help him deal with his anger by nurturing his unique musical talents. Drama, starring Dustin Hoffman, Kathy Bates, Debra Winger, Eddie Izzard and Garrett Wareing.(n)

programme.title: Diary of a Wimpy Kid: The Long Haul (2017)
programme.desc: Youngster Greg Heffley hatches a plot to re-route a family road trip towards a videogame convention. Comedy sequel, starring Jason Drucker, Alicia Silverstone and Tom Everett Scott.(n)

programme.title: Home Alone 2: Lost in New York (1992)
programme.desc: Hapless youngster Kevin somehow manages to catch the wrong plane and finds himself heading for New York, while his family jets off to Florida. Having got his hands on his father's credit card, he is enjoying the good life staying in a luxury hotel - until he bumps into two familiar burglars with an axe to grind. Comedy sequel, starring Macaulay Culkin, Joe Pesci, Daniel Stern, Brenda Fricker and Tim Curry.(n)

programme.title: The Way Way Back (2013)
programme.desc: An introverted teenager joins his mother and her overbearing boyfriend on an awkward family holiday. He befriends a free-spirited employee at the nearby water park, and the relationship inspires him to assert his independence for the first time. Comedy drama, starring Liam James, Sam Rockwell, Steve Carell, Toni Collette and Allison Janney.(n)

programme.title: Four Weddings and a Funeral (1994)
programme.desc: A London man's misfortunes in love look set to change when he meets an American beauty. However, the prospect of a transatlantic relationship succeeding seems slim, so the pair go their separate ways - only for him to realise he is unable to get her out of his mind. Richard Curtis's comedy, with Hugh Grant, Andie MacDowell, John Hannah, Charlotte Coleman, Kristin Scott Thomas, Simon Callow and James Fleet.(n)

programme.title: Get Out (2017)
programme.desc: After African American photographer Chris and his white girlfriend Rose visit her wealthy parents, he suspects there's more to the locals than meets the eye. Oscar-winning psychological thriller written and directed by Jordan Peele (Us), starring Daniel Kaluuya, Allison Williams, Bradley Whitford and Caleb Landry Jones.(n)

programme.title: Candyman (1992)
programme.desc: A graduate student is researching urban legends for her dissertation and investigates the tale of a supernatural, hook-handed serial killer rumoured to haunt a run-down Chicago apartment block. Though cynical at first, she soon finds herself face to face with the murderous soul, who sets his sights on claiming her as his latest victim. Horror, starring Virginia Madsen, Tony Todd and Xander Berkeley.(n)

programme.title: Animal Farm (1954)
programme.desc: Animals rebel against their unscrupulous owner and take control of his farm, vowing to run things fairly, with equality for all. However, the pigs leading the revolt succumb to greed and corruption, culminating in violence and oppression. Animated drama based on George Orwell's novel, narrated by Gordon Heath and with Maurice Denham providing the voices for all the characters.(n)

programme.title: The Return of the Pink Panther (1975)
programme.desc: The inept Inspector Clouseau sets out to unmask a notorious international jewel thief believed to be behind the theft of a priceless diamond - but anarchy, as ever, follows wherever he goes. Blake Edwards' comedy sequel, starring Peter Sellers, Christopher Plummer, Catherine Schell, Herbert Lom, Burt Kwouk and Graham Stark.(n)

programme.title: The Pink Panther Strikes Again (1976)
programme.desc: Inspector Clouseau fends off a murder syndicate led by his former police chief Dreyfus, who has been driven insane by his hatred for the bungling detective. However, doing away with the lucky lawman proves harder than anticipated - even for the world's best assassins. Comedy sequel, starring Peter Sellers, Herbert Lom, Lesley-Anne Down, Burt Kwouk and Leonard Rossiter.(n)

programme.title: The Two Faces of January (2014)
programme.desc: A conman masquerades as a tour guide in Greece, and selects a wealthy American tourist and his younger wife as likely targets. However, when a murder is committed at the couple's hotel, all three are incriminated, and a dangerous alliance develops. Thriller based on Patricia Highsmith's novel, starring Viggo Mortensen, Kirsten Dunst and Oscar Isaac.(n)

programme.title: Now You See Me (2013)
programme.desc: Four magicians use their lavish stage shows as cover for a series of seemingly impossible heists, and give away the money they steal to their audiences. An FBI agent assembles a team of experts to discover how the baffling robberies are committed and what the secret agenda behind the crime spree is. Crime thriller, starring Jesse Eisenberg, Mark Ruffalo, Woody Harrelson and Isla Fisher.(n)

programme.title: Hidden Figures (2016)
programme.desc: As the space race reaches fever pitch in the early 1960s, a trio of female African-American mathematicians attempt to overcome prejudice and find work with NASA. Their remarkable skills prove vital in getting an astronaut into orbit and bringing him safely back to Earth, but to realise their potential they must overcome deep-seated prejudice in the space programme and society as a whole. Fact-based drama, starring Taraji P Henson, Octavia Spencer, Janelle Monae and Kevin Costner.(n)

programme.title: The Last Witch Hunter (2015)
programme.desc: A medieval warrior thwarts an evil witch queen's plan to wipe out the human race, but is cursed with immortality as a result. In the modern day, he continues to battle evil, and faces his greatest challenge when the sorceress is resurrected to resume her evil plan. Fantasy adventure, starring Vin Diesel, Elijah Wood, Rose Leslie and Michael Caine.(n)

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: Audrey and Jeff argue when he confesses he does not believe she saw her dead grandmother's ghost, while Russell tries to talk Timmy out of his arranged marriage. Comedy, starring Patrick Warburton and Megyn Price.(n)

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: Audrey persuades Jeff to attend couples therapy, but the sessions prove to be unsuccessful - leaving the pair to resolve their own issues, while Russell tries to get over his crush. Comedy, starring Patrick Warburton and Megyn Price.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the West Midlands, social worker Tutsi serves up a mixture of Zimbabwean and English dishes. One of Tutsi's guests harbours a grudge about some of her previous comments, while some close-to-the-bone nicknames are thrown around the table and there's a very mixed reaction to a surprise worm-eating competition.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in the West Midlands, dark horse Digby plans to surprise his guests with an evening of fancy fine dining. However, Digby's biggest challenge proves to be maintaining harmony between his guests, with George still upset with Sunita for dissing his food and with straight-talking Tutsi trying to pay Liz a compliment which is misinterpreted.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam's plan to reignite his fading relationship with Dana by taking her to a 'Weird Al' Yankovic concert backfires. When Barry and Erica become peer counsellors at school, they worry that Murray may be feeling depressed. 'Weird Al' Yankovic and Tim Meadows guest star.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry seeks fresh inspiration when he loses his standing as a champion athlete at school and attempts to introduce a new sport to his friends. Murray refuses to let Beverly help out at the furniture shop when sales take a nosedive, but she's convinced the latest home furnishing craze holds the answer.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: As they bond over their shared love for the movie Dirty Dancing, Beverly is thrilled when Erica suggests the film as the theme for the upcoming school dance. However, Principal Ball refuses to sanction anything with the word 'dirty' in the title, threatening Erica with suspension if she persists with the idea. Barry lobbies for the dance to be based on his favourite movie Footloose, while Adam and Pops encourage Murray to learn to dance in order to make Beverly happy.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam turns to the cool new guidance counsellor for advice. Meanwhile, Erica and Geoff try to hide the fact that they are living together.(n)

programme.title: Schooled
programme.desc: Principal Glascott and Lainey struggle to agree on what is an appropriate dress code. Elsewhere, Coach Mellor struggles with his relationship with Julie.(n)

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Baby Tooth and the Egyptian God of Knowledge
programme.desc: The eccentric youngster makes a scientific breakthrough at the dentist's office. Dale and Meemaw go to the casino and Georgie makes a big mistake when he is left in charge of the store.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Tensions rise when the guys struggle to complete their Air Force project on time and are forced to work around the clock - leaving Sheldon fighting to stay awake. Penny and Amy attend a university gathering at the home of Bert the geologist. Raj learns Bernadette's true feelings about her pregnancy when he helps her to clear out the spare room for a nursery.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard and Bernadette decide they do not want to know the gender of their unborn child, but are upset when they hear Raj has found out before they have. After Amy's apartment floods, she proposes a cohabitation experiment with Sheldon to stay with him while her apartment undergoes repairs.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Things don't run smoothly as Amy and Sheldon's cohabitation experiment continues, forcing Leonard and Penny to intervene when the couple have an argument about the 'bathroom schedule'. Howard and Bernadette cancel their holiday plans when Bernadette feels unwell and decide to have a secret stay-cation at home instead. However, the pair are soon disturbed by unexpected guests dropping by.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny discovers she has a dedicated group of fans from her Serial Ape-ist days when she accompanies Leonard to a comic-con. Meanwhile, Sheldon and Amy host a brunch at Penny's flat, and stress over the baby's arrival prompts Howard to make an impulsive purchase.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After the repairs are completed in Amy's flat, she lies to Sheldon by telling him that the work is still ongoing so they can carry on living together for a while longer. Leonard is upset when he finds out Penny has been moving his collectibles out of Sheldon's old room and into storage behind his back.(n)

programme.title: Lemony Snicket's A Series of Unfortunate Events (2004)
programme.desc: Three orphans are entrusted into the care of their sinister uncle, a master of disguise who intends to kill them for the family fortune. Quickly realising he is up to no good, the children flee, moving from one prospective guardian to the next - but their wily relative is never far behind. Comedy adventure based on the children's books, starring Jim Carrey, Meryl Streep, Jude Law, Liam Aitken and Emily Browning, with Kara and Shelby Hoffman.(n)

programme.title: Batwoman
programme.desc: Kate and Sophie must reconcile with their past, Mary grapples with the fate of the Kane family as Catherine seeks Jacob's help, while Alice is in on yet another nefarious plan.(n)

programme.title: Misfits
programme.desc: A series of threatening messages leaves the teenagers worried that someone knows their secret, and Nathan discovers his mother's boyfriend sleeping outside in the nude. Curtis and Alisha continue their flirting, while Simon meets a mysterious girl on the internet, who shows an interest in his videos. Comedy drama, starring Lauren Socha, Nathan Stewart-Jarrett, Iwan Rheon, Antonia Thomas and Robert Sheehan.(n)

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson guides two more singletons on the dating show based naked attraction alone. First into the studio is 26-year-old Adele from Scotland. After beating cancer, Adele wants to live life to the full and find a guy who feels the same. She is followed by 22-year-old Jack from Manchester. After a dramatic four-stone weight-loss, Jack wants to show off his new body and attract the one for him.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp joins members of a Women's Institute group and together they learn needle felting and how to make a mosaic. However, teaming up is a first for the presenter and they all try to agree on their final entry for the New Forest and Hampshire County Show.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Widow Jenny Kevan seeks to realise her late husband's dream of buying a home near Menton in France. However, she is torn between purchasing a Gallic pad and relocating to Yorkshire to be close to her relatives. Laura Hamilton and Adrian Simpson offer their advice.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Redditch where Henry restores a 1970s Kawasaki motorbike, while Simon takes a pair of rusty cinema seat supports and creates a desirable garden bench.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Wales, where Henry restores a Royal Enfield motorbike, converting it into a classic cafe racer. Simon's choice of an old school house radiator springs a leak, but he gets an 1980s pinball machine back to its best.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: in and JB turn their attention to transforming their chateau's ruined orangery into a top-class events venue. When the concrete is delivered for a new floor, JB must race against time to find a missing piece of kit before the concrete sets. Tensions bubble to the surface as a new spa is delivered to Tim and Sasha's chateau, while Fiona celebrates her birthday and continues to battle sub-zero temperatures as she renovates a bedroom for her daughter Rochelle.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to Cambridgeshire, where first-time self-builders Mark and Helen Farmer are trying to construct a traditional home appropriate for modern living. Charlie tries to keep them focused on the details, but it's the big stuff that threatens to trip them up. When Mark and Helen break ground, they discover Roman and Anglo-Saxon artefacts on site, a surprise that threatens to bring the project to a standstill.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at The Admiral Hotel in central London, run by owner So Shaw and hotel manager Kevin Jankunas. Chris Foster and his German companion Christel Richter are floored by the London prices, and Ursula Beswick and Daria Pakalnicka question the quality of the facilities. Despite So and Kevin's best efforts to charm their guests, a dinner conversation leaves Kris Malde and Vikki Cheatham making accusations of sexism and So and Kevin are outraged by some of their feedback.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to The Redstone in Blackpool, where high-end hotelier Ursula Beswick and manager Daria Pakalnicka hope their luxury guest house will win the day.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Chris Foster and Christel Richter invite the hoteliers to Ann's Farmhouse in the suburbs of Bradford, West Yorkshire, but tensions remain high between So, Kevin, Kris and Vikki.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to the R Inn in Desborough, owned by Kris Malde. However, when simmering tensions between the guests boil over, one of them refuses to stay the night.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: As the B&B owners meet for the last time, bones of contention include a missing biscuit, marks on a toilet seat and one guest is confronted about why they refused to stay the night. There are harsh words and home truths before the overall winner is revealed.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien realise profit potential in an old crop sprayer, a cast-iron fire guard and some old wooden workshop clamps.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. Copper Beech House are first to host and Steve and Kathy want Phil to swallow his synthetic sausage comment.(n)

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates more recipes, tips and hacks tailored for the Covid-19 outbreak, here revealing how to make minestrone soup and a simple chicken curry. The chef shows us how to make frugal food nutritious and delicious with ingredients drawn entirely from the store cupboard.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Erin and JB test run the orangery as a new events space by hosting their A Midsummer Night's Dream-themed party for a group of friends visiting from the UK. In the south of France, Tim and Sasha set out to turn their chateau's original bathing pool - which has sat empty for 15 years - into a unique feature for a new wellness garden. Plus, Fiona unveils a plaque and hosts a party to celebrate her chateau's 845th birthday. To ensure the drinks are served at the right temperature, she transforms one of the chateau's old dungeons into the perfect wine cellar.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to Devon for an update on an inspirational build. Royal Marine captain Jon White and his wife Becky were living happily in a small cottage when in June 2010, while serving in Afghanistan, Jon stepped on an improvised explosive device, losing three limbs. Every part of their lives had to change - starting with where they could live. Their architect came up with a cutting-edge, semi open-plan building with a dramatic wing-like-roof - and crucially no obvious signs of adaptations for Jon's disabilities. Has the new home proved as enabling for the couple and their expanding family as they had hoped?(n)

programme.title: Thou Shalt Not Kill
programme.desc: The death of respected oncologist Francesca Molino at first suggests suicide, but Valeria realises that something is wrong. Her investigation turns to Fabio Serra, the father of one of the children that the doctor had in care. However, Fabio is not the only one with reasons to be angry with the doctor. Valeria also finds herself in a difficult position as regards Viola's care. Miriam Leone stars. In Italian.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Thirteen-year-old Tom is rushed to St George's after crashing his bike head first into a parked car while racing a friend. Doctors order a CT scan to assess his injuries and must decide whether he will need emergency surgery to his face. Plus, 26-year-old Andy comes to A&E after injuring his knee during a game of Frisbee, and 51-year-old Jason arrives after suffering from blurred vision for two days.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-five-year-old Gerry is rushed to A&E with a dangerously high heart rate of more than 250bpm, and doctors suspect an underlying chest infection is the cause. Meanwhile, 92-year-old Margaret arrives at St George's after falling over and hitting her head, and six-year-old Daniel is treated for an infected blister on his foot.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits PJ's Steakhouse in Queens, New York, to provide his expert advice to the owners. They decided to turn a successful bar into a high-end restaurant, but without any previous experience in managing a business they have found themselves seriously in debt. With an over-confident chef and a partner propping up the bar, co-owner Madalyn faces a few difficult decisions.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Thirteen-year-old Tom is rushed to St George's after crashing his bike head first into a parked car while racing a friend. Doctors order a CT scan to assess his injuries and must decide whether he will need emergency surgery to his face. Plus, 26-year-old Andy comes to A&E after injuring his knee during a game of Frisbee, and 51-year-old Jason arrives after suffering from blurred vision for two days.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-five-year-old Gerry is rushed to A&E with a dangerously high heart rate of more than 250bpm, and doctors suspect an underlying chest infection is the cause. Meanwhile, 92-year-old Margaret arrives at St George's after falling over and hitting her head, and six-year-old Daniel is treated for an infected blister on his foot.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton visits Vietnam, where a global surge in demand for pepper has transformed the lives of farmers across the country. She learns the difference between black and white varieties and the secrets of peppercorn quality control, and discovers the remarkable lengths to which producers go to guard their valuable crop from thieves. Jimmy Doherty meets a man whose job entails harvesting different strains of yeast from locations around the world and Matt Tebbutt looks into how manufacturers flavour crisps.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits the Nidderdale agricultural show in the Yorkshire Dales, where she learns how to make her own pork pies and sausages. However, her success jeopardised by problems the evening before the event.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin come to the assistance of Cyndy Bevan, who cannot decide where to spend her retirement, and faces a choice between Lynton in north Devon or Aude in south-central France.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Irum and her husband Murtaza have a family nest egg to invest in a property, but need to decide whether they want to stay close to their Midlands base or relocate to the Costa Almeria in Spain. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin show the couple what they could buy in each location.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Heather and Nigel Holmes fancy a brand-new life in a modern property with amazing views in the Mijas area of Spain. However, as Heather's mum is in a home in Hampshire, they would also like a smaller base near to her for when they visit the UK. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin help them find the best of both.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with two couples who knew exactly where they wanted to live. Katrina and Nick were looking for their first place together as a couple on the small but pricey island of Jersey, while Helen and Rick had a search area of just four square miles in the popular Manchester suburbs of Hale and Altrincham, where they were hoping to find a family home.(n)

programme.title: Phil Spencer's Stately Homes
programme.desc: Phil is invited by Lord David Cholmondeley to visit Houghton Hall in Norfolk, built for Britain's first prime minister Robert Walpole, and attempts to discover how much it cost. He also discovers the story of a disreputable member of the Walpole family who sold the art collection and even the front steps of the house to pay his gambling debts.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Julie hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in Co Durham, hoping to win over her guests with a no-nonsense menu she can prepare in advance. Joining her are tough-talking prison transport officer Tina, dreadlocked farm-dweller Stephen, superior IT manager David and glamorous beauty-school teacher Ashleigh. Alliances are quickly forged when David and Tina discover they both love a good gossip, before they turn their attention to Stephen when David compares him to a tramp. Julie overcooks her beef, but will her hosting make up for it and bag her the £1,000 prize?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty teacher Ashleigh throws the second of the week's parties in Co Durham, she hopes a menu of sophisticated dishes - along with a helping of hearts and love - will help her win the cash prize. However, cracks were already beginning to show in the diners' genial facade yesterday, and the hostess realises she must play peacemaker if she is to keep everyone from falling out.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Call centre complaints officer and part-time farmer Stephen hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Co Durham. He hopes to win the £1,000 prize money by impressing his guests with an evening on his farm, featuring a drinks reception and rustic cuisine. However, self-confessed snob Tina makes it perfectly clear what she thinks of his efforts.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Prison transport officer Tina is the fourth host in Co Durham. She hopes to impress her guests with a sophisticated French menu including salmon crepes, chicken and posh peas and pear dessert. But having been highly critical of everyone else's food so far, will they now take the chance to get their own back or can she win them round?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final evening of gatherings in Co Durham sees IT manager David stage a French-themed soiree in the hope of securing the £1,000 prize. However, he soon finds his main challenge is to keep all his guests on friendly terms.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Steve and Beverly Green welcome their guests to Centery Farm, Devon, where one contestant decides to keep her past as a hotel inspector secret. The couple's afternoon herding activity is thwarted by heavy rain and fog, and the following morning's feedback showdown has repercussions that will be felt all week.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Professional chef Ben Cooke invites the contestants to the Little Gloster on the Isle of Wight, where he hopes to impress them with a taste of Scandinavian living. Assisted by his 81-year-old grandmother Lillian, who has more than 50 years' experience in the hotel industry, the host splits the visitors into two teams for a contest, but simmering tensions quickly reach boiling-point, resulting in a bitter snub at dinner.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Stephen Hammon hosts the third day of the competition at the four-star Old Hall House B&B in Warwickshire, providing his guests with a variety of on-site activities including shooting and archery before taking them for a meal at his own restaurant. However, he ends up trying too hard to impress at breakfast the next day after Sandie De Rougemont reveals her past as a hotel inspector.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Ex-hotel inspector Sandie De Rougemont, owner of the House of Agnes in Canterbury, plays host. She takes her guests to a local brewery and an upmarket restaurant, where everyone seems to enjoy themselves, but the revelation that a ghost may roam the rooms of the B&B leaves some of the participants nervous.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Stuart and Rosie Treasurer from the Wirral plan to transform a small bungalow from a 1960s dormer into a slick piece of 21st-century architecture. The couple decapitate the building and balance a new floating timber box on top to create five bedrooms, spending as little as possible on plumbing, electrics and insulation, but stress levels spiral when neighbours grumble at the ultra-modern creation.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 43-year-old cyclist is brought to A&E after crashing face first into a car, with injuries are so severe that he was put into an induced coma at the scene. Meanwhile, 68-year-old Pat awaits the results of an X-ray after injuring her leg on holiday, and 89-year-old Adrian complains of chest pain.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Beth, 68, is rushed to St George's after falling down a flight of stairs and badly breaking her ankle, meaning she faces emergency surgery and intensive physiotherapy to walk again. Leigh, 67, is admitted with head injuries after being knocked off his bicycle in a hit-and-run incident and medics are concerned he has suffered a severe trauma to his head as he was not wearing a helmet, while 26-year-old Luke arrives in A&E after an accident at work involving an electric saw.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The first episode of the hit comedy. The Craggy Island cleric dreams of achieving stardom on the Faith of Our Fathers TV show - but his hopes are dashed by Jack and Dougal's untimely intervention. Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Frank Kelly star.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: A sense of dread descends on Craggy Island as the priests prepare for poker-faced Father Stone's annual visit - and Ted tries desperately to think of a scheme to get rid of him. Clerical comedy, starring Dermot Morgan.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the popular quiz as Sean Lock and Roisin Conaty compete against Jon Richardson and Nish Kumar. Adam Buxton joins the show's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 43-year-old cyclist is brought to A&E after crashing face first into a car, with injuries are so severe that he was put into an induced coma at the scene. Meanwhile, 68-year-old Pat awaits the results of an X-ray after injuring her leg on holiday, and 89-year-old Adrian complains of chest pain.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Beth, 68, is rushed to St George's after falling down a flight of stairs and badly breaking her ankle, meaning she faces emergency surgery and intensive physiotherapy to walk again. Leigh, 67, is admitted with head injuries after being knocked off his bicycle in a hit-and-run incident and medics are concerned he has suffered a severe trauma to his head as he was not wearing a helmet, while 26-year-old Luke arrives in A&E after an accident at work involving an electric saw.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The first episode of the hit comedy. The Craggy Island cleric dreams of achieving stardom on the Faith of Our Fathers TV show - but his hopes are dashed by Jack and Dougal's untimely intervention. Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Frank Kelly star.(n)

programme.title: River Cottage Bites
programme.desc: Head gardener Mark Diacono provides a step-by-step guide to planning a vegetable patch, and shows how people can save money by growing their own produce, expanding their culinary horizons and providing the freshest produce around.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend join forces to tackle Tim's very own 1965 Ford Mustang, preparing for a road trip around Britain revisiting a few of their favourite cars from across the last four series. En route, they stop off at some top petrolhead pitstops, and there's a chance to enjoy unseen footage and outtakes from across the series, including a chat with actor Roger Moore.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw work on a 1971 Fiat Dino coupe for an ex-mechanic who had to put the restoration of the vehicle on hold when he was diagnosed with a rare type of cancer. During the process, Tim takes a drive in one of the most beautiful cars in the world and Fuzz gets his hands on a Ferrari racing engine for the first time.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Car-fan Tony's 1962 Jaguar Mark 2 has been in his family for well over 40 years. Due to complications following a stomach operation, he hasn't been able to restore the car himself and it has been sitting neglected in a garage for nearly two decades. With Fuzz Townshend battling to start the Jag's engine and Tim Shaw having to get his hands dirty making parts, it's a race against the clock to get this British classic finished on time.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In 2010, new parents Mary and David were looking for a home close to her sister in Cambridgeshire, while Mary's mother and father, Edna and George, were seeking out a property in the same area, hoping to live near to their children and grandchildren. Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with the family to find out whether they were able to meet the requirements of everyone involved.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer and Kirstie Allsopp catch up with two couples who were looking for family homes for life. Sunee and Will were desperate to put down permanent roots in London near Sunee's relatives, while Alison and Phil were swapping the buzz of Bolton for rural Herefordshire.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: A quartet of B&B owners battle it out to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money, beginning with luxury weight-loss and detox retreat Slimmeria in East Sussex. The guests worry about uncomfortable sleeping arrangements, the price of accommodation and the strict 9pm curfew - and the prospect of a dance-style workout only exacerbates their bad mood.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jan and William Cheeseman, owners of five-star Gold-rated 38 St Giles in Norwich, try to impress their competitors. Unfortunately, poor breakfast presentation and complaints about noise from the street increase the tension between the couple as they strive for perfection.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Ann and Bill Clay play host at their four-star Bosvean House B&B in Bude, Cornwall. Diane and Max Seivewright query the establishment's luxury description, while Galia Grainger complains about the lack of a sea view. However, the hosts hope an indulgent Cornish cream tea, a stick dance and a drink in their garden shed will succeed in winning over the guests.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Village bakers Diane and Max Seivewright, the owners of Ombersley B&B in Worcestershire, host the last round of the competition, but some guests are far from impressed with the rooms and food.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners gather to find out who has won the competition. Diane and Max Seivewright, the hosts at Ombersley B&B, feel betrayed by Ann and Bill Clay's feedback and want to know why they have been so harshly criticised, while Galia Grainger asks why Slimmeria's rules and regulations have provoked so much hostility.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sex and the City fan Jose Stewart hosts the first dinner party in Lincolnshire for teacher Skip Schultz, tattooist Indy Nijjar, entrepreneur Sharon Wright and admin worker Jessica Carroll, but struggles to concentrate on her Spanish-themed menu, opting instead to spend her time on Twitter. A game of truth or dare then brings out some interesting revelations, as well as an unlikely romantic encounter.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: History teacher Skip Schultz hosts the second party in Lincolnshire, planning an evening to celebrate his American roots. While the guests are impressed with the starter, the main course proves a confusing flop, and Skip has to rely on his entertainment to save the day.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Sharon Wright hosts Lincolnshire's third party, hoping to impress with dishes of seared scallops, crispy-top pork belly, and chocolate praline tart, which she has repeatedly practised in preparation for the evening. The businesswoman has a strict schedule and has considered her guests' every need, even hiring a gardener to ensure the yard is in full bloom.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Tattoo artist Indy Nijjar hosts a summer-themed dinner party in Lincolnshire, instructing his guests to bring swimwear with them. In a last-ditch bid to score high marks, he decides to serve his dessert in a unique place, but will have to wait and see whether it is enough to win the £1,000 prize.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Admin worker Jessica Carroll hosts the final party in Lincolnshire. Despite demonstrating a distinct lack of culinary knowledge over the course of the week, including asking teacher Skip Schultz if his pumpkin pie contained pumpkin, she hopes her Chinese meal and unusual pet will impress the guests.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows a project to renovate and extend a classic 1920s cinema in Thorne, South Yorkshire. With little knowledge of restoration, Gwyn and Kate boldly set about turning the dilapidated building into a family home using masses of concrete after taking advice from an architect friend. The couple are hoping to create a hydraulic glass wall that opens up one side of the house to form a UV-filtered canopy, and there are also plans for a white roof extension that resembles a diving board.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics treating a woman with brittle bone disease end up with another patient on their hands. A man collapses at the side of the road, but refuses to go to hospital.(n)

programme.title: Inside Birmingham Children's Hospital
programme.desc: Three families dealing with difference at Birmingham Children's Hospital, including 17-year-old Tijana, who was born with eyes that are unusually small and has reduced vision. First-time parents Charlotte and Dan have just had baby Lily, who was born with a cleft lip and palate, and against their protective instincts, they are putting her forward for corrective surgery at just six weeks old. Meanwhile, rock fan 14-year-old Ellie and her dad Gary bring their own unique energy to the Emergency Department, but Ellie's mood is volatile as she battles feelings of depression.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: With more than 88,000 under-18s arrested in 2016 in the UK, this edition focuses on how Wiltshire police are dealing with an increase in young people challenging their authority. Are young people being given too many chances or not enough?(n)
