programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: James May straps into a highly tuned hatchback as he competes in a world rallycross race, with Top Gear USA host and professional driver Tanner Foust teaming up with him. Jeremy Clarkson puts the latest Jaguar F-type R and Eagle's modernised E-type the Low Drag GT though their paces, while Richard Hammond is in Spain to test the new version of the Mazda MX-5. Plus, X-Men: First Class star Nicholas Hoult takes to the track in the Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear: Winter Blunderland
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May present their best winter challenges.(n)

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Coverage of the international event in London, as amateur drivers put their home-made, gravity-powered vehicles through an obstacle-littered course. The judges for the event at Alexandra Palace were snowboarder Katie Ormerod, racing driver Andrew Jordan, grime artist Blakie and 2015 Red Bull Soapbox Race podium finisher Tony Whitelaw, while contestants included Team Brooklands Special, 24 Seconds of Le Ally Pally and Team Rockin' Rolla.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May compare the Mercedes SLS, the Porsche 911 GT3 RS and the Ferrari 458 Italia, as they race against each other in an epic road trip up America's east coast.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a road trip, trying to follow the path of the Three Wise Men across the Middle East. However, they opt to do so in open-top sports cars that were built for significantly less demanding tasks than driving along the conflict-ridden mountains of southern Turkey, through war-ravaged Iraq and across the searing deserts of Syria. The trio also try to find out what myrrh is and where it can be bought.(n)

programme.title: Daddy Day Care (2003)
programme.desc: Two unemployed executives decide to open a day-care centre, believing they have found an easy way to make a profit - but instead discover looking after a horde of rampant kids is not as simple as it seems. Family comedy, starring Eddie Murphy, Jeff Garlin, Steve Zahn and Anjelica Huston.(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: A group of comedians completes a series of tasks. This time, Sally Phillips faces an amorous water cooler, and Aisling Bea crams a dish into a balloon.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Festive edition from 2008 in which Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on one of the most spectacular road trips they have ever attempted. programme.title: Room 101

Their gripes include new towels, escalators and the bartender at the local pub.(n)

This time, the Stephen Fry prepares for the festive period with some seasonal merriment.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Live at the Apollo Christmas Special
programme.desc: The BAFTA nominated comedy showcase returns for its third annual Christmas special, welcoming some of the biggest and best comedic talent to the stage for some Yuletide frolics. Previous editions have featured stand-up routines and compering from renowned comedians such as Ed Byrne, Nish Kumar, Rob Beckett, Sara Pascoe and Henning Wehn. This time, Canadian comedian, writer and actress Katherine Ryan hosts the show, welcoming former I'm A Celebrity contestant and stand-up comic Joel Dommett, and Bristol-born comedian and radio presenter John Robins to the stage.(n)

programme.title: Wild Africa
programme.desc: Documentary focusing the mountains of Africa, home to many rarely seen species. From the snow-covered peaks of Kilimanjaro to the Ethiopian Highlands where temperatures fall as low as -20C, wildlife continues to thrive, including the rat-catching wolves which revel in their rodent-infested territory and bone-smashing vultures with a taste for marrow. Unique habits and survival instincts are all on display as the unpredictability and numerous threats to mountain life are revealed.(n)

programme.title: Wild Africa
programme.desc: The savannah's mixture of grassy plains and woodlands boast an array of wildlife, ranging from many species of big cat to herds of buffalo, wildebeest and gazelle. The programme explores the way animals and people have shaped these lands.(n)

programme.title: Wild Africa
programme.desc: The wildlife of the African deserts, where rain may not fall for 10 years at a time. In spite of inhospitable conditions, a multitude of species survives in these arid landscapes, including cartwheeling spiders, camels, squirrels which use their tails as sunshades, and even crocodiles.(n)

programme.title: Wild Africa
programme.desc: Fergal Keane narrates a documentary focusing on the abundant wildlife found along the continent's 29,000km coastline. Species exist in habitats as diverse as the icy waters of the Cape and the tropical climes of the Indian Ocean, leading to some bizarre variations in behaviour - seals eat birds and die of heatstroke, crabs climb trees and penguins live in deserts.(n)

programme.title: Wild Africa
programme.desc: The wildlife of Africa's green belt, an area spanning 5,500km from Uganda in the east to Sierra Leone in the west, which is hotter, brighter and wetter than anywhere else on the continent, allowing plants to flourish. People and animals have learned to exploit this abundance - colobus monkeys have developed specialised bacteria-filled stomachs to neutralise the toxins found in their leafy diet, while the Bakaya and Fang tribes use plant poisons to stun their quarry when hunting.(n)

programme.title: Wild Africa
programme.desc: Documentary revealing how fresh water acts as a magnet to a variety of creatures on the world's most parched continent. Flocks of flamingos congregate on the inhospitable soda lakes of east Africa, giant crocodiles battle with buffalo on the banks of Zambia's Luangwa river, and cichlid fish use catfish as minders.(n)

programme.title: Wild Africa

programme.title: VE Day: Countdown to Peace
programme.desc: This compelling documentary celebrates the approaching 75th Anniversary of freedom and peace in Europe.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Chronicling the events that brought about the demise of the Third Reich, as the Allies arrived at the gates of Berlin and Hitler boarded himself up in his bunker, where it is believed he committed suicide. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The creation of a set of Second World War sea defences built near the mouth of the River Thames to protect London. Plus, a concrete pyramid build during the Cold War.(n)

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: Field Marshal Montgomery's daring airborne plan to end the war by Christmas, which went a bridge too far at Arnhem.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The desert town that faced a disaster of biblical proportions. Plus, scorched remains that speak of imprisonment and torture and vast coastal ruins that reveal a past once wiped from history.(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The country's diverse wildlife, beginning by exploring how rural Chinese peasants in the south have developed a relationship with the region's animal inhabitants - including how water buffalo are used in agriculture, and tamed cormorants help their owners to fish.(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The documentary series exploring China's wildlife continues with a journey to the Yunnan Province, where parallel mountain ranges channel mighty rivers from Tibet southward to the tropics, and the landscape provides a home to diverse species. While the mountain forests are home to red pandas, the valleys below contain jungles inhabited by gibbons, sunbirds and elephants.(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Wildlife living on the Tibetan plateau - an inhospitable area the size of western Europe - including brown bears, yaks, giant sheep, snow leopards and the chiru or Tibetan antelope.(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Examining the heartland of the country, home to ancient structures such as the Great Wall and modern buildings like Beijing's Olympic Stadium, as well as animals including the giant panda and golden snub-nosed monkey. However, the region holds even deeper cultural significance as the land of origin of China's most populous ethnic group the Han people - whose 5,000-year-old traditions are increasingly under threat.(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Built to shelter ancient China from hostile barbarians to the north, the Great Wall also serves as a cultural curtain, beyond which can be found a variety of traditions. This programme goes outside the Wall to meet tribes of reindeer herders on the Siberian border, and explores deserts to the north where ruined cities were once bustling staging posts for camel trains on the legendary Silk Road.(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The series concludes with a visit to the country's eastern seaboard to investigate how the Chinese government is managing environmental concerns while meeting the ever-expanding needs of the population. Among the region's diverse locales lie futuristic cities, seaweed-thatched villages and rugged wetlands that are home to some of the world's rarest animal species.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Celebration of the decade's psychedelic music, featuring counter-culture classics by the Who, Pink Floyd, Joe Cocker, the Nice and the Jimi Hendrix Experience.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: The late 1960s saw the first regular transmission of colour television, and the music world followed the trend with the launch of BBC television show Colour Me Pop. This episode features some of the programme's highlights, including performances by the Who, the Kinks, the Small Faces, the Bonzo Dog Doo-Dah Band and the Jimi Hendrix Experience.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Celebration of the supergroups and guitar heroes to emerge toward the end of the decade, including Pink Floyd, the Rolling Stones, Fleetwood Mac and Jimi Hendrix.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Seventies
programme.desc: Studio and concert performance from the decade, featuring the Who, Queen, David Bowie, the Moody Blues, the Rolling Stones and Elton John.(n)

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part two of two. Experts analyse the Princess's marriage to Lord Snowdon at the start of the 1960s. At home with celebrities and artists, they're riding the wave of a cultural and sexual revolution that's transforming Britain. The mystique of monarchy is being increasingly undermined by the emergence of a more open and egalitarian society.(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Castle
programme.desc: The Knights of the Garter arrive at Windsor Castle for their annual lunch with members of the monarchy, which includes a complicated procession through the palace. The royal archives allow cameras to view nearly 200 years of photo albums, including the thousands of photographs from the Queen Mother's collection, and the castle clock-maker faces a race to ensure all 450 timepieces on the estate are turned back.(n)

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn visits the Ffestiniog in North Wales originally built to transport slate from mountain to coast and learns about Amsterdam Centraal.(n)

programme.title: VE Day: Countdown to Peace
programme.desc: This compelling documentary celebrates the approaching 75th Anniversary of freedom and peace in Europe.(n)

programme.sub-title: George III and the Prince Regent
programme.desc: Tracy Borman investigates the troubled relationship between George III and his eldest son, and the impact the king's declining mental health had on the prince.(n)

programme.title: Private Lives of the Monarchs
programme.desc: The life of Louis XIV, revealing the unpleasant truth behind the grandeur of the Palace of Versailles, and exploring the king's love life and unusual fashion sense.(n)

programme.desc: Billy, Gwendoline and Michael locate their heating problem, but it means Billy faces the tricky task of repointing the chimney 20 metres up - despite being scared of heights.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Presenters Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig welcome comedians Joe Lycett and Lee Mack, writer and actor Griff Rhys Jones, and TV presenter Melanie Sykes to the famous tent. The celebrities face three tricky tests set by judges Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood - beginning with a blondies signature challenge, then a testing technical and concluding with the opportunity to reveal their secret passions with their cake showstopper. Which of the famous faces will impress those judges?(n)

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Maitre d' Fred Sirieix and his staff take care of another group of singletons hoping to find love while staying in a luxury hotel. Last week saw online acquaintances Georgia and Rachael meet by chance at the hotel pool. Now they are on dates with their male matches - only for one person to be left with a difficult dilemma that could result in a broken heart. Meanwhile, antiques dealer Alex and opera singer Hannah check in for their romantic rendezvous, and sparks fly when cab driver Josie meets plumber Martin.(n)

programme.title: Joe Lycett's Got Your Back
programme.desc: Once again, the comedian becomes the peoples' champion in another instalment of this series investigating buyers' rights and justice, this week focusing on the efficient removal and recycling of waste. Actor and comedian Guz Khan lends Joe a hand as he takes aim against fly-tippers - and chucks council rubbish collections in the bin. Comedian Rosie Jones exposes the recycling shame of one of Britain's largest cinema chains, while Mark Silcox tests out Pizza Hut's all-you-can-eat buffet promotion.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Egypt's Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: An archaeological team makes a discovery that exposes a state-sanctioned looting scandal, while in Cairo, experts in antiquities work with Interpol to crack down on the black market trade in stolen relics. A dig run by Spanish archaeologists makes an unusual discovery - a 3000-year-old mummified heart.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Theatre enthusiast Jay Coleman hosts the first party in Newport, Gwent, putting on a dramatic evening with an international menu, in the hope of winning the £1,000 prize at the end of the week.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Peace activist Pippa Bartolotti hosts the second of the week's dinner parties in Newport, Gwent, serving up a menu of locally sourced vegetarian food and hoping to entertain her guests by indulging in a spot of fortune-telling. However, a talkative Duncan Powell threatens to dominate proceedings.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Radio journalist Emma Jones hosts the third dinner party in Newport, Gwent, serving up an American-themed menu of crab cakes and pulled pork, and the 23-year-old tries to boost her chances of taking home the prize money with a cheerleading session.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Recruitment boss Duncan Powell hosts the final dinner party in Newport, Gwent, and hopes his Hawaiian-themed evening will impress his guests enough to win the prize. Unfortunately for him, prawn brains and sea bass bones harm his chances of success, so the confident cook tries to rescue the evening with a lemon posset.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples in north Somerset show off their culinary and hosting skills, Norman and Dee kick things off with an Indian menu that proves too spicy for their guests, followed by young lovebirds Ashley and Sophie - although Ashley lets the side down with an un-ironed shirt. Finally, self-styled funnyman Brett and his wife Vicky end the week with an American menu.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

He also samples the buffet on a bullet train, discovers why Okinawa is embracing American fast food, and visits the ancient capital of Kyoto, where he attempts to develop a taste for tofu.(n)

programme.title: Walking with Elephants
programme.desc: Explorer, conservationist, author and presenter Levison Wood treks alongside hundreds of elephants on their annual migration across Botswana. He gets up close and personal for the first time with a group of orphans and takes part in an ancient ceremony, before setting off into the bush in search of a herd to follow. Following potentially dangerous encounters with a buffalo and a pride of lions, Lev finds his first herd, but is forced to leave them alone when one threatens to charge.(n)

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door

Stewart McCracken and Elmor Victor face a heartbreaking house repossession in Merseyside, where two landlords are forced to evict a tenant recovering from a brain tumour which has cost him his job and income.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: After Charlie runs into Chelsea and Brad at the movies he asks Alan to go with him to her house to spy on the couple, but when Brad does goes home for the night and Chelsea drives to Malibu, the pair realise she is driving to Charlie's house. However, the next morning Charlie realises she never intended to get back together with him and he goes to Tom and Ed's house for advice.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie hooks up with Chelsea's best friend, which causes problems when his ex suggests a reconciliation, and Alan has an unexpected effect on a new female patient.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan plans an intimate night at a hotel with his partner, while Charlie tries a different approach to get the 'girlfriend experience'.(n)

programme.sub-title: Three Girls and a Guy Named Bud
programme.desc: Jake discovers that Alan has been secretly dating Eldridge's mother Lindsey, and is so furious he decides to move out and live with Judith full time. Meanwhile, Charlie resolves to drop his booze habit.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.sub-title: A Bottle of Wine and a Jackhammer
programme.desc: Alan struggles to decide whether to move in with Lyndsey, having become comfortable staying at Charlie's place. However, when he discovers that Judith is not so keen on the idea of his living so close, he gleefully takes the plunge - just to provoke his ex-wife's temper.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Despite now living with Lindsey, Alan hooks up with an ex-girlfriend, prompting his eternally put-upon brother to pick at his conscience. However, an accident involving a discarded pipe places Charlie at the mercy of his sibling once again. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Rose makes one of her dramatic balcony entrances, interrupting Charlie's romantic evening with Michelle. She proceeds to reveal that she is about to get married, prompting Alan and his long-suffering brother to crash the event. However, things are not quite what they seem.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie and Alan hope to cheer up a depressed Evelyn by inviting Russell the pharmacist to her birthday party. However, when he shows up with a hot date in tow, the Harper matriarch ends up even more upset.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Ben try to drill through the locks on a property to evict the residents, only to find to their surprise that the door has been modified with special reinforced metal. Elsewhere, Delroy and Brian try to reclaim money a garage owes to a customer and are shocked by the condition of the vehicle in question, and a woman who has acted as guarantor for her sister's tenancy does not realise the extent of her responsibilities.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement agents evict a large group of squatters barricaded into a disused nursery in London, and have to resort to smashing their way in as they refuse to leave. Meanwhile, agents in Bristol try to recover over £10,000 owed by a plumbing business to a supplier, and emotions run high when they attempt to evict a tenant who pleads her innocence in West London.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Vic become party to a fractured family's school fee woes in Staffordshire and Del and Dael need to reclaim £3,500 after a rental van was returned badly damaged. Stewart and Elmor are in Warwickshire, where the members of a household blame their predicament on a scheming landlord whose subsequent arrival sparks a fierce reaction, while Iain helps Stewart remove a young mother from her Lancashire home.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The agents chase almost £3,000 owed by Indian takeaway owners, but when the staff deny any knowledge of the debt, the agents threaten to seize kitchen equipment. In east London they face a race against time to recover more than £8,000 from an electrical services company, and in Cheshire the enforcement agents chase nearly £4,000 of debt owed by a mobile phone shop.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The housing team in Harrow accompany the police on a raid after receiving reports of dangerous conditions at a home belonging to a notorious slumlord's brother. Lettings agent John wants to turn a former Blackpool hotel into bedsits, but a routine check on the property reveals not only a severely neglected house, but also a drugs den littered with paraphernalia and some of the worst conditions he has ever witnessed.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Letting agent Paul uncovers a cannabis farm during a routine arrears inspection, but learns that the tenant has been in prison for the past four months and claims ignorance of the criminal crop. Meanwhile, builders Stuart and Steve win a possession order to regain their property, but face the worst damage they have ever seen and a £20,000 bill to repair it.(n)

programme.desc: A woman living in Slough becomes embroiled in a dispute with a neighbour, certain that he is behind recent damage to her car and stones thrown at her window. But things go from bad to worse as Gordon and Manny end up fighting in the garden.(n)

programme.desc: Briscoe and Green question a comedian in connection with the death of his adopted child, and uncover allegations that he molested an 11-year-old boy years previously. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Logan uncover hidden motives when a paedophile is murdered on live TV by the father of one of his victims. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Christopher Noth.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine who appears to have been leading a double life is shot dead. As the investigation continues, however, the team uncovers a case of identity theft - together with a tangled motive involving a mysterious package and a wronged wife. Drama, starring Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A six-year-old boy begs the team to find his father who, he claims, has been kidnapped. programme.desc: Jessica visits a friend's Arizona ranch - but as soon as she arrives, the community is plunged into disarray by a local psychic's sinister predictions. Whodunit, starring Angela Lansbury.(n)

programme.desc: The investigation into the rape and murder of an undercover federal informant leads the team to an online ring of paedophiles and a case of international teenage prostitution. Guest starring Pam Grier.(n)

programme.desc: The dishevelled detective suspects a highly respected general is guilty of murdering an associate who was about to expose the war hero's shady business dealings, but Columbo has a hard time backing up his hunch with evidence because of the lack of a body or a weapon. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Eddie Albert, Suzanne Pleshette and John Kerr.(n)

Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Fisher Stevens and Molly Hagan.(n)

programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. programme.title: Witness
programme.desc: Documentary. Mohammed Yusef wants to build his own radio station inside the world's largest refugee camp at Cox's Bazaar to give Rohingya refugees a voice.(n)

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life.(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: Dr Frank Enstine erases Kylie and Kim's memories, and Kate has to remind them who they are in time for a concert.(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The band suffers a run of mishaps while in Acapulco, and discovers that a shaman has accidentally cursed them with bad luck.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Gilby challenges Vic to prove his courage by spending the night alone in a forest full of hungry wolves.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar is challenged to a race by a haughty rival captain with a spectacular boat - but Vic hatches a plan to give the Drakkar an extra burst of speed.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The lively zebra suggests arranging a Present Day on the island, but struggles when he is asked to find a gift for Vicky.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra realises that the island needs tidying up and organises a scavenger hunt to make everyone participate.(n)

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Lotus starts to turn into a bamboo plant after being bitten by a snake, and Tashi and Jake must find a cure before the Baron cuts down the whole bamboo forest.(n)

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: A magic carpet sends Jack and Tashi back to prehistoric times. When they return, they discover they have changed history so monkeys now rule the world.(n)

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life.(n)

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement men who parachuted with the SAS into France on D-Day and the massacre of 35 men in a French woodland.(n)

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams reveals how as Hitler's plans to invade Britain took shape, members of Churchill's Resistance Movement got ready to defend the nation, consulting their specially-prepared instruction manuals on explosives and killing techniques.(n)

programme.title: Fighting for King and Empire: Britain's Caribbean Heroes
programme.desc: During the Second World War, thousands of men and women from Britain's Caribbean colonies volunteered to join the fight against Hitler, but their contribution has largely been forgotten. In this documentary based on the film Divided By Race - United in War and Peace, veterans tell their extraordinary wartime stories and reveal how they have faced a lifelong struggle to be treated as equals by the UK government and the British people.(n)

programme.title: Nuremberg - Nazis on Trial
programme.desc: Dramatisation of the trial of Hitler's deputy, Rudolf Hess, the third in line to leadership of Nazi Germany, who was declared insane by the Fuehrer after making an unexpected errand of peace to Britain in 1941. The senior Nazi's fitness to stand trial was at the centre of his case in the tribunal. Narrated by Matthew MacFadyen and starring Ben Cross as Hess.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: A look at individuals who played their part in creating a more culturally diverse post-war Britain, including pioneering actor Earl Cameron, who broke through the colour bar in the film industry. Other well-known names also considered are gay rights activist George Montague, and Brian Rix, who has campaigned passionately on the issue of disability. Plus, Arctic Convoy survivor Austin Byrne and former prisoner of war Jim Purcell share their stories and memories of the era.(n)

programme.title: Elenya (1992)
programme.desc: Aa 12-year-old half-Italian Welsh schoolgirl stumbles across a wounded German airman during the Second World War and secretly cares for him - only for tragedy to strike as the local townsfolk discover what she has been up to. Drama, starring Pascale Delafourge Jones, Klaus Behrendt, Margaret John and Sue Jones Davies.(n)

programme.title: The Purple Plain (1954)
programme.desc: A Canadian squadron leader's increasingly strange behaviour begins to worry the officers of an RAF station deep in the Burmese jungle. There is further concern when the unstable pilot crashes his plane miles from the base, forcing him to make an exhausting trek back to safety. Second World War drama, starring Gregory Peck, Bernard Lee, Brenda de Banzi and Maurice Denham.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Featuring Woolwich, Deptford and Lewisham. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Sarah Beeny follows a woman who plans to live with her family in the property she wants to develop, and keep a close eye on her £400,000 dream. But as the project progresses, she loses control of both the development and the budget, putting the entire undertaking at risk of failure.(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: A bomb is found in a nightclub which German scientists claim cannot be disarmed, and Ash receives a mysterious message. Guest starring Judy Geeson (the original 1970s Poldark).(n)

programme.title: D-Day: The Shortest Day
programme.desc: Survivors provide a compelling insight into the D-Day landings, discussing the preparations, their actions, and the consequences of an operation which had such a crucial bearing on the outcome of the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: Boy Soldiers of WWII
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the truth behind the boys who lied about their age in order to enlist and fight in the Second World War, and their shocking stories from the frontline.(n)

programme.title: Golden Years (2016)
programme.desc: A retired couple face a grim future after a financial crisis costs them both their pensions. Refusing to take the situation lying down, they resolve to take back what is rightfully theirs with a series of daring bank robberies. Comedy, starring Bernard Hill, Virginia McKenna, Simon Callow and Sue Johnston.(n)

programme.title: The English Teacher (2013)
programme.desc: A teacher is reunited with her favourite past pupil, who has returned from New York having failed to succeed as a playwright and under pressure from his family to get a job. She puts her reputation on the line to prove her protege has talent by getting the school to put on a production of his play. Comedy drama, starring Julianne Moore, Michael Angarano and Greg Kinnear.(n)

programme.title: Undercover Hooligan (2016)
programme.desc: Suspended cop Michael Clarke is tasked with infiltrating an infamous London gang and bringing down its leader. British crime drama, starring Kris Johnson and Patrick Connolly.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Exploring the districts of Woolwich, Plumstead and Deptford.(n)

programme.title: Blitz Spirit (2019)
programme.desc: Short film set during the London Blitz, in which a pianist struggles to justify his war efforts to his stubborn mother.(n)

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: Cabbie Joey Bone road tests three fish and chip restaurants, Kerryann Dunlop whips up London's best pizza, and there is a trip to see some cured meats down a cobbled lane.(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks alongside a canal in Wolverhampton and to the top of Sour Howes in the Lake District.(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks to the top of Caer Caradoc in Shropshire and Zoe Dawes visits Grasmere in the Lake District.(n)

programme.title: Talking Points
programme.desc: A chance to catch up on debates about the issues behind the headlines.(n)

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life.(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls attend the unveiling of their waxworks at a London museum - but a set of mystical bracelets bring the models to life.(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The band gets trapped in the Bermuda Triangle while heading for Jamaica.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar receives a message in a bottle containing a plea for help from a damsel in distress, and Vic realises the ship is infested with woodworm.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: The crew is kidnapped by a tribe of British warrior women, who force Halvar to prove he is innocent of plundering their kingdom by undertaking four challenges.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra wants Zara and Lawrence to take a nap so they can watch the shooting stars later that night.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra discovers a variety of flowers on the island and decides to collect them all.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar and his crew throws all the food overboard to appease a monster they think is chasing them - but then have to go ashore in dangerous territory to find more.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: A band of Welsh druids threatens to shave off Halvar's beard as an offering to the gods, but a game of dominoes may hold the key to defeating them.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra has fun getting his trousers dry on washing day.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The lively zebra and his friends perform in a musical concert.(n)

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to harass the enemy, recalling how, as Hitler prepared to invade, the secret army went underground along the south and East Anglian coasts.(n)

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the women who were recruited to join the British Resistance movement and helped deceive Hitler into thinking the D-Day Allied invasion of France would come across the Straits of Dover.(n)

programme.title: Churchill's First World War
programme.desc: Drama-documentary charting Winston Churchill's road to redemption after his disgrace following the Dardanelles disaster during the First World War through intimate letters he wrote to his wife Clementine. It reveals how he was instrumental in the creation of the tank and notes his contribution to the Allied victory in 1918 as Minister of Munitions, demonstrating how the future prime minister was shaped by his experiences of the Great War.(n)

programme.title: D-Day: The Shortest Day
programme.desc: Survivors provide a compelling insight into the D-Day landings, discussing the preparations, their actions, and the consequences of an operation which had such a crucial bearing on the outcome of the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: Victim (1961)
programme.desc: A secretly gay barrister discovers his lover has been driven to suicide by blackmailers, who have also extorted money from several other men. Risking his reputation and marriage, he hunts down the culprits, fully aware that it could lead to his own exposure as a homosexual, illegal at the time. Drama, starring Dirk Bogarde, Sylvia Syms, Dennis Price and Anthony Nicholls.(n)

programme.title: Very Important Person (1961)
programme.desc: A pompous Allied scientist ends up in a PoW camp after crash-landing during a reconnaissance mission over Germany. His uncompromising attitude and a series of misunderstandings initially earn him the enmity of his fellow inmates, but his popularity improves when he devises an audacious escape plan. Second World War comedy, with James Robertson Justice, Stanley Baxter, Leslie Phillips and Eric Sykes.(n)

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Sarah Beeny follows the trials and tribulations of Kim Maoate, who gave up a lucrative job in IT to remortgage her Buckinghamshire home and buy a dilapidated property 170 miles away in South Wales. The house was bought unseen and it needs more work than she ever imagined. A week into the project, her friend still hasn't shown up to lend a helping hand and the costs are starting to rise.(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Ash gets some time off and plans to spend it in bed - but Susan has other ideas.(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: The health advice series returns with a New Year special, in which Dr Saleyha Ahsan oversees a pioneering experiment that aims to see how people can burn more fat without doing any more exercise. Medical journalist Michael Mosley offers the cheat's guide to gaining a better body, and Dr Chris van Tulleken puts protein shakes to the test. Plus, surgeon Gabriel Weston meets a woman whose sight was restored thanks to a bionic eye.(n)

programme.title: Hitler on Trial: The Story Behind the Film
programme.desc: Documentary about the Jewish lawyer Hans Litten who put Adolf Hitler on the witness stand in 1931, in the hopes of exposing the truth about his dangerous character.(n)

programme.title: Fox
programme.desc: Final episode of the drama following a London gangster and his family. Starring Peter Vaughan, Ray Winstone and Bernard Hill. Originally broadcast in 1980.(n)

programme.title: K-Shop (2016)
programme.desc: A Bournemouth takeaway owner despairs of his drunken, abusive customers and is finally pushed to the limit when his father is killed in a violent altercation. He begins murdering the people who make his life a misery, and disposes of the bodies by making them into kebabs. Horror, starring Reece Noi and Ewen MacIntosh.(n)

programme.title: Spivs (2004)
programme.desc: A professional con-man spends his time honing the skills of his protege and making sure his gang of conniving criminals stays out of trouble. However, when a simple heist backfires the ragtag group are left with a lorry-load of illegal immigrants on their hands - and an angry smuggler with revenge on his mind soon adds to their woes. Crime drama, starring Ken Stott,, Nick Moran and Dominic Monaghan.(n)

programme.title: Golden Years (2016)
programme.desc: A retired couple face a grim future after a financial crisis costs them both their pensions. Refusing to take the situation lying down, they resolve to take back what is rightfully theirs with a series of daring bank robberies. Comedy, starring Bernard Hill, Virginia McKenna, Simon Callow and Sue Johnston.(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: The health advice series returns with a New Year special, in which Dr Saleyha Ahsan oversees a pioneering experiment that aims to see how people can burn more fat without doing any more exercise. Medical journalist Michael Mosley offers the cheat's guide to gaining a better body, and Dr Chris van Tulleken puts protein shakes to the test. Plus, surgeon Gabriel Weston meets a woman whose sight was restored thanks to a bionic eye.(n)

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: Cab driver Joey Bone samples three London beers, and Kerryann Dunlop prepares her version of fried chicken, while Jemma Wilson indulges her sweet tooth with a trio of treats.(n)

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: John Quilter selects his favourite moments from the series that cuts straight to the core of London's food scene.(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: First episode of the drama about a military bomb disposal team working in London during the blitz. New recruit Brian Ash is thrown in at the deep end. Starring Anthony Andrews.(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Ash employs some new technology that does not work. A new recruit gets some training, and a magazine article causes tension amid the team's superior officers.(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Ash and Machin must dispose of a bomb in a school - only to find another device close by, forcing them to split up and defuse one each.(n)

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Britain stood alone in 1940, facing the might of the all-conquering German forces, but Winston Churchill had an idea. He set up an Underground Army, of farmers, teenagers, women and veterans from WW1.(n)

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Britain stood alone in 1940, facing the might of the all-conquering German forces, but Winston Churchill had an idea. He set up an Underground Army, of farmers, teenagers, women and veterans from WW1.(n)

programme.title: D-Day: The Shortest Day
programme.desc: Survivors provide a compelling insight into the D-Day landings, discussing the preparations, their actions, and the consequences of an operation which had such a crucial bearing on the outcome of the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: Hitler on Trial: The Story Behind the Film
programme.desc: Documentary about the Jewish lawyer Hans Litten who put Adolf Hitler on the witness stand in 1931, in the hopes of exposing the truth about his dangerous character.(n)

programme.title: Battle of Britain: The Real Story
programme.desc: James Holland explores the Battle of Britain from the German point of view. The author focuses on the tactics, technology and intelligence available to both nations, and examines how they decided to use their resources. He also gains access to first-hand testimonies from German pilots, and diaries and documents that reveal a previously unknown side to this confrontation.(n)

programme.title: We Dive at Dawn (1943)
programme.desc: A British submarine crew is unexpectedly ordered to carry out a top-secret mission to sink the German fleet's latest battleship in the Baltic Sea. But it is not long before disaster strikes and the vessel becomes stranded in the middle of the ocean with no fuel or food. Second World War adventure, starring John Mills, Eric Portman, Reginald Purdell and Niall MacGinnis.(n)

programme.title: Hell is Sold Out (1951)
programme.desc: An author long assumed dead makes a surprise return, only to find books he did not write are being published under his name. He discovers that another writer, claiming to be his widow, is passing off her own creations as his previously unseen work to get them published. Drama, starring Mai Zetterling, Herbert Lom and Richard Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing New Cross, Eltham and Catford. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: A property developer's plans for a hi-tech bachelor pad in Battersea grind to a halt after he starts building without his neighbours' permission. Sarah Beeny follows Jonathan Moon's progress after he is forced to pay thousands in compensation and sees his costs spiral out of control, forcing him to give up his own home and car.(n)

programme.title: Blitz Spirit (2019)
programme.desc: Short film set during the London Blitz, in which a pianist struggles to justify his war efforts to his stubborn mother.(n)

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: As the destruction caused by Hitler's V1s in London and the south of England reached critical proportions, a new fighter was introduced to challenge the enemy's flying bombs. Wing Commander 'Bee' Beaumont remembers his first encounters with the Tempest, and the Wilkinson family from Croydon share their account of life on the ground with help from their day-to-day diary.(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Dr Chris Van Tulleken runs a groundbreaking experiment with the University of Surrey to explore whether changing mealtimes can promote weight loss, while Gabriel Weston looks at whether diet products can cause people to put weight on. Plus, an investigation into the chemicals sprayed around houses, the truth about alcohol, and the story of a 12-year-old girl undergoing a new treatment for a peanut allergy.(n)

programme.title: Golden Years (2016)
programme.desc: A retired couple face a grim future after a financial crisis costs them both their pensions. Refusing to take the situation lying down, they resolve to take back what is rightfully theirs with a series of daring bank robberies. Comedy, starring Bernard Hill, Virginia McKenna, Simon Callow and Sue Johnston.(n)

programme.title: Active Measures (2018)
programme.desc: Director Jack Bryan addresses the allegations of collusion between the Trump campaign and agents of the Russian state.(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner.(n)

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings.(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong quizzes another line-up of famous faces on the general knowledge game show. The teams are former England international rugby union stars Ben Cohen and Kyran Bracken, children's TV stalwarts Helen Skelton and Dani Harmer, The Office actors Patrick Baladi and Ewen MacIntosh, and EastEnders stars Cheryl Fergison and Ricky Groves. Who'll be best when it comes to picking the most obscure correct responses? Richard Osman is the one with the answers.(n)
